- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- spec-human: Create a human-reviewable architecture spec using a 6-section template (Summary, Background, Architecture, Interface, Edge Cases, Alternatives) BEFORE implementing a non-trivial design. Triggered explicitly via /spec-human <topic>, or when the user says "write a human spec", "create a review-ready design doc", "spec this out for review", or "I need a spec for X before we build it". Produces an ASCII markdown document at ./specs/<slug>.md aimed at peer engineers and tech leads who need to validate core architecture and trade-offs in 5 minutes. Do NOT auto-trigger on general design questions or coding tasks - this is a deliberate review gate that only fires on explicit request. For LLM-targeted specs (future sibling skill), use /spec-llm instead.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# ROADMAP: Relocate cross-cutting e2e tests to top-level `hybrid_sim/`
2	
3	Tracks the migration of the e2e test tree (fixtures, harness, drivers, plugin
4	shell tests, demos) out of the `verilator/` submodule and into the top-level
5	`hybrid_sim/` repo, so the wire format (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`) and the
6	tests that exercise it live in the same repo.
7	
8	One session = one feature. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
9	completion (done).
10	
11	## Status Legend
12	
13	- not-started: feature has not begun
14	- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
15	- done: structural+behavioral commits landed and Phase-0 baseline pass list matches
16	
17	## Global Invariant
18	
19	After every feature lands, this must hold:
20	
21	```
22	JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh
23	# pass list == /tmp/baseline-pass.txt from F0
24	```
25	
26	Any divergence blocks the next feature.
27	
28	## Dependency DAG
29	
30	```
31	F0 ----> F1 ----> F2 ----> F3 ----> F4 ----> F5 ----> F6 ----> F7
32	                  (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR
33	                   sub-loop inside F2)
34	```
35	
36	Strict serial order from F1..F6. F7 (docs) can land any time after F6.
37	
38	## Pre-flight decisions (answer before F1)
39	
40	1. **History preservation** - cross-repo `cp` loses per-file `git blame` in the
41	   new home. If blame matters, do F1 via `git filter-repo --path hybrid/test
42	   --path tests/hybrid` on a clone of `verilator` and merge with
43	   `--allow-unrelated-histories`. Adds ~half a day. Default: plain copy,
44	   accept that blame for moved files starts fresh in `hybrid_sim/`.
45	2. **Submodule CI** - any pipeline in `verilator` that runs
46	   `ctest -L hybrid-e2e` silently passes with 0 tests after F4. Decide:
47	   delete the CI job, or have it clone the parent repo.
48	3. **`runtime/` headers** - confirm `core_v5.h`, `memory_map.h`, `platform.h`
49	   under `verilator/hybrid/test/runtime/` are standalone fixture files, not
50	   sync-mirrors of vsim CPU description headers. If they mirror, add a
51	   regenerate step or keep them in the submodule.
52	
53	## Features
54	
55	### F0 - Capture baseline pass list
56	
57	- Status: done
58	- Type: no commit (safety net)
59	- Description: Snapshot the current green test list so every later phase can
60	  diff against it. Runs both CSR and PC trigger modes.
61	- Steps:
62	  ```
63	  JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh 2>&1 | tee /tmp/e2e-baseline.log
64	  sed 's/\x1b\[[0-9;]*m//g' /tmp/e2e-baseline.log | grep '^\[ok\]'   | sort > /tmp/baseline-pass.txt
65	  sed 's/\x1b\[[0-9;]*m//g' /tmp/e2e-baseline.log | grep '^\[FAIL\]' | sort > /tmp/baseline-fail.txt
66	  ```
67	  (sed strips ANSI color codes the runner emits.)
68	- Output: `/tmp/baseline-pass.txt` is the diff target for F1..F6.
69	- Result (captured 2026-05-18): 42 PASS / 3 FAIL.
70	  - The 3 failing tests are pre-existing breakage orthogonal to this
71	    migration: `rt_no_qemu_binary`, `rt_gdbstub_timeout`, `rt_pty_handback`
72	    use `-M virt -kernel` against fixtures now linked for `andes_ae350`,
73	    so QEMU1 never reaches the `csrwi` trigger and the drivers hang
74	    until killed. F1..F6 only need to preserve the 42 PASS; the 3 FAIL
75	    stay FAIL.
76	- Dependencies: none
77	- TDD RED: n/a (no code change).
78	
79	### F1 - Lay down top-level `tests/` tree (copy-only)
80	
81	- Status: done
82	- Type: structural (top-level repo)
83	- Description: Copy the e2e tree into `hybrid_sim/tests/` WITHOUT touching the
84	  originals in the submodule and WITHOUT touching `scripts/run_e2e.sh`. Both layouts
85	  coexist after this commit. Old path is still authoritative.
86	- Target layout:
87	  ```
88	  hybrid_sim/tests/
89	    e2e.sh           <- cp verilator/tests/hybrid/e2e.sh
90	    fixtures/        <- cp -r verilator/hybrid/test/       (Makefile, *.S, *.c, runtime/, test.ld, semihost_exit.inc)
91	    drivers/         <- cp verilator/tests/hybrid/rt_{corrupt_state,gdbstub_timeout,no_qemu_binary,pty_handback}.sh
92	    plugin/          <- cp test_plugin_*.sh probe_vcsr_drain.sh qemu_silent_stub.py
93	    demos/           <- cp demo_*.sh
94	  ```
95	- Key files:
96	  - `tests/e2e.sh` (new, identical bytes to submodule original)
97	  - `tests/fixtures/*` (new)
98	  - `tests/drivers/*.sh` (new)
99	  - `tests/plugin/*.sh` (new)
100	  - `tests/demos/*.sh` (new)
101	- Dependencies: F0
102	- TDD RED: n/a - pure copy, no behavior change. Verification = baseline pass
103	  list still matches (`scripts/run_e2e.sh` still uses old paths).
104	- Commit: `structural: copy e2e harness + fixtures to top-level tests/`
105	
106	### F2 - Rewire path resolution in new copies (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR)
107	
108	- Status: in-progress
109	- Type: behavioral (top-level repo)
110	- Description: The copied `tests/e2e.sh` still computes
111	  `REPO_ROOT=$SCRIPT_DIR/../..` which assumes it lives inside the submodule,
112	  so its `VSIM_BIN`/`VSIM_PLUGIN`/`VSIM_ELF` paths are wrong from the new
113	  location. Drive the fix by TDD: failing smoke test first, then minimal edit

115	- Sub-features (each = one commit):

120	  - **F2b (GREEN) [done, 48b3fe7]**: Edit `tests/e2e.sh` minimally so `TOP_ROOT=$SCRIPT_DIR/..`
121	    and the four resolved paths point at top-level locations:
122	    | Path        | New value                                            |
123	    |-------------|------------------------------------------------------|
124	    | `VSIM_BIN`    | `$TOP_ROOT/verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium`    |
125	    | `VSIM_QEMU`   | `$TOP_ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64`           |
126	    | `VSIM_PLUGIN` | `$TOP_ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`         |
127	    | `VSIM_ELF`    | `$TOP_ROOT/tests/fixtures/${FIXTURE}.elf`            |
128	    - Commit: `behavioral: resolve harness paths from top-level tests/ root`
129	  - **F2c (REFACTOR) [done, 0829018]**: Extract the now-duplicated env-var resolution block
130	    into `tests/lib/paths.sh`; source it from `e2e.sh`. Baseline still green.
131	    - Commit: `structural: dedupe harness path resolution into tests/lib/paths.sh`
132	  - **F2d**: Apply the same RED/GREEN/REFACTOR cycle to `drivers/rt_*.sh`,
133	    `plugin/test_plugin_*.sh`, `plugin/probe_vcsr_drain.sh`, `demos/demo_*.sh`.
134	    Each file family = one commit using the F2c helper.
135	    - Commits:
136	      - `behavioral: rewire negative drivers (drivers/rt_*.sh) to top-level paths` [done, 98fc5b7]
137	      - `behavioral: rewire plugin-only shell tests to top-level paths`
138	      - `behavioral: rewire switching-mode demo scripts to top-level paths`
139	- Key files:

142	  - `tests/drivers/rt_*.sh`

144	  - `tests/demos/demo_*.sh`
145	  - `tests/smoke_e2e_paths.sh` (new, deleted at end of F2 or kept as part of suite)
146	- Dependencies: F1

148	
149	### F3 - Point `scripts/run_e2e.sh` at the new tree
150	
151	- Status: not-started
152	- Type: behavioral (top-level repo)
153	- Description: Switch the parallel runner's `HARNESS` and `TEST_DIR` variables
154	  to the new top-level locations. Fixture-build call moves from
155	  `make -C verilator/hybrid/test` to `make -C tests/fixtures`. After this
156	  commit, the old paths inside `verilator/` are unreachable from

158	- Key files:
159	  - `scripts/run_e2e.sh:24-25` (HARNESS, TEST_DIR)
160	  - `scripts/run_e2e.sh` fixture-build invocation
161	- Dependencies: F2
162	- TDD RED: `JOBS=8 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh` already covers the change - run it
163	  before the edit, expect identical pass list to baseline (old paths still
164	  work); run it after, expect identical pass list (new paths take over).

174	  Keep every `vsim_add_test(test_*` doctest registration - those are the
175	  hybrid-layer unit tests and they stay in the submodule (they use
176	  `recording_bus.hpp` duck-types, depend on vsim's `vsim_add_test` macro, and

186	
187	### F5 - Delete originals from submodule
188	
189	- Status: not-started
190	- Type: structural (verilator repo)
191	- Description: Two commits, separated by file family, so each is independently
192	  revertible.
193	- Steps:
194	  - **F5a**: `git rm -r hybrid/test` (Andes-toolchain fixture sources +

203	- Key files (all deleted):
204	  - `verilator/hybrid/test/` (entire directory)
205	  - `verilator/tests/hybrid/e2e.sh`
206	  - `verilator/tests/hybrid/rt_*.sh`

216	- Status: not-started
217	- Type: structural (top-level repo)
218	- Description: Update the `verilator` submodule pointer in `hybrid_sim/` to

221	- Steps:

226	  ```
227	- Key files:
228	  - `verilator` (submodule pointer)
229	- Dependencies: F5
230	- TDD RED: n/a. Verification: `JOBS=8 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh` matches
231	  `/tmp/baseline-pass.txt` from F0; `ctest --test-dir verilator/build
232	  -L hybrid-unit` matches pre-migration unit-test list.
233	
234	### F7 - Docs
235	
236	- Status: not-started
237	- Type: structural (top-level repo)
238	- Description: Update every doc that references the old path layout. Touch
239	  only files that actually contain a stale path - do not rewrite unrelated
240	  prose.
241	- Key files:
242	  - `CLAUDE.md` - "Key Files" table (rewrite the `verilator/...` entries
243	    that moved); "Test Fixtures" section path; "Running Tests" section if
244	    it cites the submodule path
245	  - `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` - fixture-build invocation, e2e invocation
246	  - `README.md` - any path references
247	  - `ROADMAP.md` / `TEST_ROADMAP.md` - mark this migration as complete if

249	  - `e2e_ROADMAP.md` - mark all F0..F7 as done
250	- Dependencies: F6
251	- TDD RED: n/a (docs).
252	- Commit: `structural: docs: reflect relocated e2e tree`
253	
254	## Compound-effect payoff
255	
256	After F7:
257	- Adding a new fixture = 1 commit in `hybrid_sim/`. No submodule pointer
258	  bump cascade across `QEMU`/`verilator`.
259	- e2e harness edits don't leak to other consumers of `verilator` (e.g. the
260	  standalone vsim demo).
261	- `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` (top-level) and `tests/fixtures/` (top-level)

263	- The submodule shrinks to its actual responsibility: vsim sources + hybrid
264	  orchestration headers + hybrid-layer unit tests.

file.content:
# ROADMAP: Relocate cross-cutting e2e tests to top-level `hybrid_sim/`

Tracks the migration of the e2e test tree (fixtures, harness, drivers, plugin
shell tests, demos) out of the `verilator/` submodule and into the top-level
`hybrid_sim/` repo, so the wire format (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`) and the
tests that exercise it live in the same repo.

One session = one feature. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: feature has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: structural+behavioral commits landed and Phase-0 baseline pass list matches

## Global Invariant

After every feature lands, this must hold:

```
JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh
# pass list == /tmp/baseline-pass.txt from F0
```

Any divergence blocks the next feature.

## Dependency DAG

```
F0 ----> F1 ----> F2 ----> F3 ----> F4 ----> F5 ----> F6 ----> F7
                  (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR
                   sub-loop inside F2)
```

Strict serial order from F1..F6. F7 (docs) can land any time after F6.

## Pre-flight decisions (answer before F1)

1. **History preservation** - cross-repo `cp` loses per-file `git blame` in the
   new home. If blame matters, do F1 via `git filter-repo --path hybrid/test
   --path tests/hybrid` on a clone of `verilator` and merge with
   `--allow-unrelated-histories`. Adds ~half a day. Default: plain copy,
   accept that blame for moved files starts fresh in `hybrid_sim/`.
2. **Submodule CI** - any pipeline in `verilator` that runs
   `ctest -L hybrid-e2e` silently passes with 0 tests after F4. Decide:
   delete the CI job, or have it clone the parent repo.
3. **`runtime/` headers** - confirm `core_v5.h`, `memory_map.h`, `platform.h`
   under `verilator/hybrid/test/runtime/` are standalone fixture files, not
   sync-mirrors of vsim CPU description headers. If they mirror, add a
   regenerate step or keep them in the submodule.

## Features

### F0 - Capture baseline pass list

- Status: done
- Type: no commit (safety net)
- Description: Snapshot the current green test list so every later phase can
  diff against it. Runs both CSR and PC trigger modes.
- Steps:
  ```
  JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh 2>&1 | tee /tmp/e2e-baseline.log
  sed 's/\x1b\[[0-9;]*m//g' /tmp/e2e-baseline.log | grep '^\[ok\]'   | sort > /tmp/baseline-pass.txt
  sed 's/\x1b\[[0-9;]*m//g' /tmp/e2e-baseline.log | grep '^\[FAIL\]' | sort > /tmp/baseline-fail.txt
  ```
  (sed strips ANSI color codes the runner emits.)
- Output: `/tmp/baseline-pass.txt` is the diff target for F1..F6.
- Result (captured 2026-05-18): 42 PASS / 3 FAIL.
  - The 3 failing tests are pre-existing breakage orthogonal to this
    migration: `rt_no_qemu_binary`, `rt_gdbstub_timeout`, `rt_pty_handback`
    use `-M virt -kernel` against fixtures now linked for `andes_ae350`,
    so QEMU1 never reaches the `csrwi` trigger and the drivers hang
    until killed. F1..F6 only need to preserve the 42 PASS; the 3 FAIL
    stay FAIL.
- Dependencies: none
- TDD RED: n/a (no code change).

### F1 - Lay down top-level `tests/` tree (copy-only)

- Status: done
- Type: structural (top-level repo)
- Description: Copy the e2e tree into `hybrid_sim/tests/` WITHOUT touching the
  originals in the submodule and WITHOUT touching `scripts/run_e2e.sh`. Both layouts
  coexist after this commit. Old path is still authoritative.
- Target layout:
  ```
  hybrid_sim/tests/
    e2e.sh           <- cp verilator/tests/hybrid/e2e.sh
    fixtures/        <- cp -r verilator/hybrid/test/       (Makefile, *.S, *.c, runtime/, test.ld, semihost_exit.inc)
    drivers/         <- cp verilator/tests/hybrid/rt_{corrupt_state,gdbstub_timeout,no_qemu_binary,pty_handback}.sh
    plugin/          <- cp test_plugin_*.sh probe_vcsr_drain.sh qemu_silent_stub.py
    demos/           <- cp demo_*.sh
  ```
- Key files:
  - `tests/e2e.sh` (new, identical bytes to submodule original)
  - `tests/fixtures/*` (new)
  - `tests/drivers/*.sh` (new)
  - `tests/plugin/*.sh` (new)
  - `tests/demos/*.sh` (new)
- Dependencies: F0
- TDD RED: n/a - pure copy, no behavior change. Verification = baseline pass
  list still matches (`scripts/run_e2e.sh` still uses old paths).
- Commit: `structural: copy e2e harness + fixtures to top-level tests/`

### F2 - Rewire path resolution in new copies (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR)

- Status: in-progress
- Type: behavioral (top-level repo)
- Description: The copied `tests/e2e.sh` still computes
  `REPO_ROOT=$SCRIPT_DIR/../..` which assumes it lives inside the submodule,
  so its `VSIM_BIN`/`VSIM_PLUGIN`/`VSIM_ELF` paths are wrong from the new
  location. Drive the fix by TDD: failing smoke test first, then minimal edit
  to make it green, then dedupe.
- Sub-features (each = one commit):
  - **F2a (RED) [done, 3b95927]**: Add `tests/smoke_e2e_paths.sh` that calls the new
    `tests/e2e.sh` with `FIXTURE=rt_c_hello TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Expect failure
    because path resolution lands in the wrong directory.
    - Commit: `behavioral: add failing smoke test for top-level tests/e2e.sh`
  - **F2b (GREEN) [done, 48b3fe7]**: Edit `tests/e2e.sh` minimally so `TOP_ROOT=$SCRIPT_DIR/..`
    and the four resolved paths point at top-level locations:
    | Path        | New value                                            |
    |-------------|------------------------------------------------------|
    | `VSIM_BIN`    | `$TOP_ROOT/verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium`    |
    | `VSIM_QEMU`   | `$TOP_ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64`           |
    | `VSIM_PLUGIN` | `$TOP_ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`         |
    | `VSIM_ELF`    | `$TOP_ROOT/tests/fixtures/${FIXTURE}.elf`            |
    - Commit: `behavioral: resolve harness paths from top-level tests/ root`
  - **F2c (REFACTOR) [done, 0829018]**: Extract the now-duplicated env-var resolution block
    into `tests/lib/paths.sh`; source it from `e2e.sh`. Baseline still green.
    - Commit: `structural: dedupe harness path resolution into tests/lib/paths.sh`
  - **F2d**: Apply the same RED/GREEN/REFACTOR cycle to `drivers/rt_*.sh`,
    `plugin/test_plugin_*.sh`, `plugin/probe_vcsr_drain.sh`, `demos/demo_*.sh`.
    Each file family = one commit using the F2c helper.
    - Commits:
      - `behavioral: rewire negative drivers (drivers/rt_*.sh) to top-level paths` [done, 98fc5b7]
      - `behavioral: rewire plugin-only shell tests to top-level paths`
      - `behavioral: rewire switching-mode demo scripts to top-level paths`
- Key files:
  - `tests/e2e.sh`
  - `tests/lib/paths.sh` (new)
  - `tests/drivers/rt_*.sh`
  - `tests/plugin/*.sh`
  - `tests/demos/demo_*.sh`
  - `tests/smoke_e2e_paths.sh` (new, deleted at end of F2 or kept as part of suite)
- Dependencies: F1
- TDD RED: F2a writes the failing smoke test before any non-test edit.

### F3 - Point `scripts/run_e2e.sh` at the new tree

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral (top-level repo)
- Description: Switch the parallel runner's `HARNESS` and `TEST_DIR` variables
  to the new top-level locations. Fixture-build call moves from
  `make -C verilator/hybrid/test` to `make -C tests/fixtures`. After this
  commit, the old paths inside `verilator/` are unreachable from
  `scripts/run_e2e.sh` but still physically present (cleanup is F5).
- Key files:
  - `scripts/run_e2e.sh:24-25` (HARNESS, TEST_DIR)
  - `scripts/run_e2e.sh` fixture-build invocation
- Dependencies: F2
- TDD RED: `JOBS=8 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh` already covers the change - run it
  before the edit, expect identical pass list to baseline (old paths still
  work); run it after, expect identical pass list (new paths take over).
  No new test file needed; the suite itself is the test.
- Commit: `behavioral: point scripts/run_e2e.sh at top-level tests/ tree`

### F4 - Drop e2e CTest block from submodule

- Status: not-started
- Type: structural (verilator repo, not top-level)
- Description: In `verilator/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`, remove the
  `if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID) ... foreach(entry ${VSIM_E2E_FIXTURES}) ...` block.
  Keep every `vsim_add_test(test_*` doctest registration - those are the
  hybrid-layer unit tests and they stay in the submodule (they use
  `recording_bus.hpp` duck-types, depend on vsim's `vsim_add_test` macro, and
  don't cross the QEMU/vsim boundary).
- Key files:
  - `verilator/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` (delete the e2e foreach block,
    keep all `vsim_add_test` lines)
- Dependencies: F3
- TDD RED: n/a - CTest registration removal. Verification:
  `ctest --test-dir verilator/build -L hybrid-unit` runs the same doctest
  set as before; `ctest -L hybrid-e2e` returns 0 tests.
- Commit (in submodule): `structural: drop e2e CTest registration (moved to parent)`

### F5 - Delete originals from submodule

- Status: not-started
- Type: structural (verilator repo)
- Description: Two commits, separated by file family, so each is independently
  revertible.
- Steps:
  - **F5a**: `git rm -r hybrid/test` (Andes-toolchain fixture sources +
    `runtime/`)
    - Commit: `structural: remove e2e fixtures (moved to parent)`
    - Verify: `ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-unit` still green.
  - **F5b**: `git rm tests/hybrid/{e2e.sh,rt_*.sh,test_plugin_*.sh,probe_vcsr_drain.sh,demo_*.sh,qemu_silent_stub.py}`
    - Commit: `structural: remove e2e shell drivers (moved to parent)`
    - Verify: `ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-unit` still green; remaining
      files under `tests/hybrid/` are only doctest `.test.cpp` units + the

  - `verilator/tests/hybrid/test_plugin_*.sh`
  - `verilator/tests/hybrid/probe_vcsr_drain.sh`

- Dependencies: F4
- TDD RED: n/a - deletion. Submodule CI's unit-test job is the regression net.

### F6 - Submodule pointer bump

- Status: not-started
- Type: structural (top-level repo)
- Description: Update the `verilator` submodule pointer in `hybrid_sim/` to

- Steps:
  ```

  git add verilator
  git commit -m "structural: bump verilator after e2e relocation"
  ```

- Dependencies: F5
- TDD RED: n/a. Verification: `JOBS=8 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh` matches

### F7 - Docs

- Status: not-started

- Description: Update every doc that references the old path layout. Touch

## Compound-effect payoff

After F7:
- Adding a new fixture = 1 commit in `hybrid_sim/`. No submodule pointer

file.content:
# Shared path resolution for the top-level e2e harness + drivers.
# Sourced (not executed). Provides:
#   TOP_ROOT                  - absolute path of the repo root
#   hybrid_resolve_paths()    - sets VSIM_BIN, VSIM_QEMU, VSIM_PLUGIN,
#                               VSIM_ELF from TOP_ROOT (unless already
#                               exported by caller) and exits 77 (CTest
#                               skip) if any artifact is missing. Reads
#                               FIXTURE for the VSIM_ELF default.

TESTS_LIB_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
TOP_ROOT=$(cd -- "$TESTS_LIB_DIR/../.." && pwd)

if [ -z "${HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN:-}" ] && [ -f "$TOP_ROOT/config.env" ]; then
  . "$TOP_ROOT/config.env"
fi

hybrid_resolve_paths() {
  VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
  VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$TOP_ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
  VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$TOP_ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
  VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$TOP_ROOT/tests/fixtures/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
  for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
    if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
      echo "skip: $f not built; run 'source ./setup.sh' and 'make -C tests/fixtures'"
      exit 77
    fi
  done
}

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# F1 contract: QEMU plugin exits with code 200 on drain (matches vsim's
3	# kExitHandoffToQemu). 0 is reserved for "workload completed normally".
4	# The orchestrator (F2+) uses this distinction to decide the next spawn.
5	#
6	# Required environment (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
7	#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64
8	#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so
9	#   VSIM_ELF     a CSR-trigger fixture (default: handoff_roundtrip.elf)
10	
11	set -euo pipefail
12	
13	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
14	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
15	
16	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
17	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
18	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"
19	
20	for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
21	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
22	    echo "skip: $f not built"
23	    exit 77
24	  fi
25	done
26	
27	STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
28	QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
29	trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$QEMU_LOG"' EXIT
30	
31	set +e
32	"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
33	  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
34	  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
35	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
36	RC=$?
37	set -e
38	
39	if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
40	  echo "FAIL[plugin_drain_exit_code]: expected QEMU exit=200 on drain, got $RC"
41	  echo "--- qemu log ---"
42	  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
43	  exit 1
44	fi
45	
46	if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
47	  echo "FAIL[plugin_drain_exit_code]: state file is empty"
48	  exit 1
49	fi
50	
51	echo "[plugin_drain_exit_code] PASS: QEMU exit=$RC, state size=$(wc -c <"$STATE_QEMU") bytes"
52

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# F1 contract: QEMU plugin exits with code 200 on drain (matches vsim's
# kExitHandoffToQemu). 0 is reserved for "workload completed normally".
# The orchestrator (F2+) uses this distinction to decide the next spawn.
#
# Required environment (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF     a CSR-trigger fixture (default: handoff_roundtrip.elf)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$QEMU_LOG"' EXIT

set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_drain_exit_code]: expected QEMU exit=200 on drain, got $RC"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_drain_exit_code]: state file is empty"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[plugin_drain_exit_code] PASS: QEMU exit=$RC, state size=$(wc -c <"$STATE_QEMU") bytes"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# F7 contract: QEMU plugin BBV-emit mode. With plugin args
# bbv_out=PATH,slice=N the plugin writes one SimPoint-format BBV row
# per N retired insns into PATH. Combine with icount=M for a clean
# exit at M insns; expected row count = M/N.
#
# Test binary: icount_spin.elf (tight 2-insn loop; predictable BBV
# pattern; CSR-less so no other trigger fires). Plugin must:
#   1. Accept bbv_out= and slice= args.
#   2. Emit M/N rows into the .bb file before drain.
#   3. Each row starts with 'T' (SimPoint .bb format prefix).
#   4. The shipped SimPoint binary (tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint) parses
#      the file without error.
#
# Required environment (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF      a CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)
#   SIMPOINT_BIN  the built simpoint clustering binary (optional;
#                 if missing the SimPoint-parse step is skipped)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
TOP_ROOT=$(cd -- "$REPO_ROOT/.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/icount_spin.elf}"
SIMPOINT_BIN="${SIMPOINT_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint}"

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

SLICE=1000
ICOUNT=10000
EXPECTED_ROWS=$((ICOUNT / SLICE))

SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-bbv-shared.XXXXXX)
BBV_OUT=$(mktemp -t bbv.XXXXXX.bb)
STATE_OUT=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
SIMPTS=$(mktemp -t simpts.XXXXXX)
WEIGHTS=$(mktemp -t weights.XXXXXX)
SP_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$BBV_OUT" "$STATE_OUT" "$QEMU_LOG" "$SIMPTS" "$WEIGHTS" "$SP_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_OUT,icount=$ICOUNT,bbv_out=$BBV_OUT,slice=$SLICE" \
  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_bbv_emit]: expected QEMU exit=200, got $RC"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$BBV_OUT" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_bbv_emit]: BBV file is empty: $BBV_OUT"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

ROWS=$(wc -l < "$BBV_OUT")
if [ "$ROWS" -ne "$EXPECTED_ROWS" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_bbv_emit]: expected $EXPECTED_ROWS BBV rows, got $ROWS"
  head -5 "$BBV_OUT"
  exit 1
fi

# Every row must start with 'T' (SimPoint .bb format prefix).
NON_T_ROWS=$(grep -cv '^T' "$BBV_OUT" || true)
if [ "$NON_T_ROWS" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_bbv_emit]: $NON_T_ROWS rows do not start with 'T'"
  exit 1
fi

# If SimPoint binary is built, verify it parses the .bb file and
# produces non-empty simpts/weights output.
if [ -x "$SIMPOINT_BIN" ]; then
  # For tiny BBVs, both dim and maxK must be conservative.
  DIM=2
  MAXK=2
  set +e
  "$SIMPOINT_BIN" -loadFVFile "$BBV_OUT" -maxK "$MAXK" -dim "$DIM" \
    -saveSimpoints "$SIMPTS" -saveSimpointWeights "$WEIGHTS" \
    >"$SP_LOG" 2>&1
  SP_RC=$?
  set -e

  if [ "$SP_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "FAIL[plugin_bbv_emit]: simpoint failed to parse BBV (rc=$SP_RC)"
    tail -20 "$SP_LOG"
    exit 1
  fi

  if [ ! -s "$SIMPTS" ] || [ ! -s "$WEIGHTS" ]; then
    echo "FAIL[plugin_bbv_emit]: simpoint produced empty output"
    exit 1
  fi
  SP_NOTE="simpoint OK"
else
  SP_NOTE="simpoint not built; parse check skipped"
fi

echo "[plugin_bbv_emit] PASS: rows=$ROWS (target $EXPECTED_ROWS), $SP_NOTE"

2	# F5 contract: QEMU plugin icount-trigger mode. With plugin arg `icount=N`
3	# the plugin drains and exits 200 after N retired insns - even on a binary
4	# that never executes the magic CSR. Foundation for SimPoint slice-consume
5	# (F11) and the orchestrator's icount-based switching (F8).
6	#
7	# Test binary: icount_spin.elf (no csrwi 0x7C0; linked at 0x80000000 for
8	# andes_ae350). Today's plugin would run forever; the new icount path
9	# must fire the drain.
10	#
11	# Required environment (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
12	#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64
13	#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so
14	#   VSIM_ELF     a CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)
15	
16	set -euo pipefail
17	
18	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
19	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
20	
21	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
22	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
23	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/icount_spin.elf}"
24	
25	for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
26	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
27	    echo "skip: $f not built"
28	    exit 77
29	  fi
30	done
31	
32	ICOUNT_TARGET=100000
33	SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
34	SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-icount-shared.XXXXXX)
35	STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
36	QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
37	trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$QEMU_LOG"' EXIT
38	
39	truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"
40	
41	set +e
42	timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
43	  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
44	  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
45	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
46	  -nographic \
47	  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
48	  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
49	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU,icount=$ICOUNT_TARGET" \
50	  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
51	RC=$?
52	set -e
53	
54	if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
55	  echo "FAIL[plugin_icount_trigger]: expected QEMU exit=200 on drain, got $RC"
56	  echo "--- qemu log ---"
57	  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
58	  exit 1
59	fi
60	
61	if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
62	  echo "FAIL[plugin_icount_trigger]: state file is empty"
63	  exit 1
64	fi
65	
66	# minstret lives at offset 632 (state_abi_check.h:35), little-endian uint64.
67	MINSTRET=$(python3 -c "
68	import struct, sys
69	with open('$STATE_QEMU', 'rb') as f:
70	    f.seek(632)
71	    print(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0])
72	")
73	
74	if [ "$MINSTRET" -lt "$ICOUNT_TARGET" ]; then
75	  echo "FAIL[plugin_icount_trigger]: minstret=$MINSTRET < icount=$ICOUNT_TARGET"
76	  echo "--- qemu log ---"
77	  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
78	  exit 1
79	fi
80	
81	echo "[plugin_icount_trigger] PASS: QEMU exit=$RC, minstret=$MINSTRET (target=$ICOUNT_TARGET)"

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# F11 contract: QEMU plugin BBV slice-consume mode. With plugin args
3	# slice=N,slice_at=K the plugin counts slice boundaries (one per N
4	# retired insns) and drains at the end of slice index K (0-based). For
5	# K=2, drain fires at insn 3*N -- the boundary that closes the third
6	# slice. Used by the orchestrator (F8) to chain spawn invocations and
7	# produce one snapshot per SimPoint cluster representative slice.
8	#
9	# Test binary: icount_spin.elf (CSR-less, predictable tight loop).
10	# Plugin must:
11	#   1. Accept slice= and slice_at= args.
12	#   2. Drain at insn (slice_at+1)*slice (the END of slice index
13	#      slice_at, 0-based).
14	#   3. Exit 200 (drain) before any icount fallback fires.
15	#
16	# Required environment (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
17	#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64
18	#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so
19	#   VSIM_ELF     CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
24	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
25	
26	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
27	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
28	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/icount_spin.elf}"
29	
30	for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
31	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
32	    echo "skip: $f not built"
33	    exit 77
34	  fi
35	done
36	
37	SLICE=1000
38	SLICE_AT=2                                # 0-based: third slice
39	ICOUNT_FALLBACK=10000                     # safety upper bound
40	EXPECTED_MIN=$(( (SLICE_AT + 1) * SLICE )) # = 3000
41	
42	SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
43	SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-phase-shared.XXXXXX)
44	STATE_OUT=$(mktemp)
45	QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
46	trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_OUT" "$QEMU_LOG"' EXIT
47	
48	truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"
49	
50	set +e
51	timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
52	  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
53	  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
54	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
55	  -nographic \
56	  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
57	  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
58	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_OUT,slice=$SLICE,slice_at=$SLICE_AT,icount=$ICOUNT_FALLBACK" \
59	  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
60	RC=$?
61	set -e
62	
63	if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
64	  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: expected QEMU exit=200, got $RC"
65	  echo "--- qemu log ---"
66	  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
67	  exit 1
68	fi
69	
70	if [ ! -s "$STATE_OUT" ]; then
71	  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: state file is empty"
72	  exit 1
73	fi
74	
75	# Verify drain timing via the plugin's own slice counter, not minstret
76	# (which is a cycle-counter proxy on andes-ax45mpv). At drain the plugin
77	# logs `idx=N target=K`; for slice_at=K, N must be K+1 (slices fully
78	# retired). slice_at=2 -> idx=3.
79	EXPECTED_IDX=$((SLICE_AT + 1))
80	if ! grep -qE "slice-consume drain \(idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT\)" "$QEMU_LOG"; then
81	  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: did not find drain at idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT"
82	  echo "--- qemu log ---"
83	  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
84	  exit 1
85	fi
86	
87	# Sanity: drained PC should be inside icount_spin's tight loop body
88	# (0x100004 addi, 0x100008 j). Anything outside means drain fired in the
89	# wrong place. pc lives at state-file offset 16 (state_abi_check.h:18).
90	PC=$(python3 -c "
91	import struct
92	with open('$STATE_OUT', 'rb') as f:
93	    f.seek(16)
94	    print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0]))
95	")
96	if [ "$PC" != "0x100004" ] && [ "$PC" != "0x100008" ]; then
97	  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: drained pc=$PC not in icount_spin loop body"
98	  echo "--- qemu log ---"
99	  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
100	  exit 1
101	fi
102	
103	echo "[plugin_phase_consume] PASS: drain idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT pc=$PC"
104

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# F11 contract: QEMU plugin BBV slice-consume mode. With plugin args
# slice=N,slice_at=K the plugin counts slice boundaries (one per N
# retired insns) and drains at the end of slice index K (0-based). For
# K=2, drain fires at insn 3*N -- the boundary that closes the third
# slice. Used by the orchestrator (F8) to chain spawn invocations and
# produce one snapshot per SimPoint cluster representative slice.
#
# Test binary: icount_spin.elf (CSR-less, predictable tight loop).
# Plugin must:
#   1. Accept slice= and slice_at= args.
#   2. Drain at insn (slice_at+1)*slice (the END of slice index
#      slice_at, 0-based).
#   3. Exit 200 (drain) before any icount fallback fires.
#
# Required environment (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF     CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/icount_spin.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

SLICE=1000
SLICE_AT=2                                # 0-based: third slice
ICOUNT_FALLBACK=10000                     # safety upper bound
EXPECTED_MIN=$(( (SLICE_AT + 1) * SLICE )) # = 3000

SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-phase-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_OUT=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_OUT" "$QEMU_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_OUT,slice=$SLICE,slice_at=$SLICE_AT,icount=$ICOUNT_FALLBACK" \
  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: expected QEMU exit=200, got $RC"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_OUT" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: state file is empty"
  exit 1
fi

# Verify drain timing via the plugin's own slice counter, not minstret
# (which is a cycle-counter proxy on andes-ax45mpv). At drain the plugin
# logs `idx=N target=K`; for slice_at=K, N must be K+1 (slices fully
# retired). slice_at=2 -> idx=3.
EXPECTED_IDX=$((SLICE_AT + 1))
if ! grep -qE "slice-consume drain \(idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT\)" "$QEMU_LOG"; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: did not find drain at idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

# Sanity: drained PC should be inside icount_spin's tight loop body
# (0x100004 addi, 0x100008 j). Anything outside means drain fired in the
# wrong place. pc lives at state-file offset 16 (state_abi_check.h:18).
PC=$(python3 -c "
import struct
with open('$STATE_OUT', 'rb') as f:
    f.seek(16)
    print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0]))
")
if [ "$PC" != "0x100004" ] && [ "$PC" != "0x100008" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: drained pc=$PC not in icount_spin loop body"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[plugin_phase_consume] PASS: drain idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT pc=$PC"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Test: QEMU plugin drains vxsat / vxrm / vcsr into the state file.
#
# Runs the probe_v_csr fixture (sets vxrm=1, vxsat=1 before QEMU1 drain)
# and verifies the state binary holds those values at the ABI-locked offsets.
#
# Offsets from state_abi.h / state_abi_check.h (HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX=64):
#   vxsat = 1320 + 32*64 + 0  = 3368
#   vxrm  = 1320 + 32*64 + 8  = 3376
#   vcsr  = 1320 + 32*64 + 16 = 3384
#
# Expected values after the fixture: vxsat=1, vxrm=1, vcsr=3
#   vcsr = (vxrm << 1) | vxsat = (1 << 1) | 1 = 3
#
# Returns 0 (PASS) or 1 (FAIL). Exit code 77 skips when artifacts are absent.

set -euo pipefail

ELF_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../../hybrid/test" && pwd)"
PLUGIN_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../../../qemu_plugin" && pwd)"
QEMU="${QEMU:-$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../../../build/qemu" && pwd)/qemu-system-riscv64}"

ELF="$ELF_DIR/probe_v_csr.elf"
PLUGIN="$PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"

[[ -x "$QEMU" ]]    || { echo "SKIP: no QEMU at $QEMU";   exit 77; }
[[ -f "$PLUGIN" ]]  || { echo "SKIP: no plugin at $PLUGIN"; exit 77; }
[[ -f "$ELF" ]]     || { echo "SKIP: no ELF at $ELF";     exit 77; }

STATE=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE"' EXIT

# Plugin exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) on drain; see F1 in ROADMAP.md.
set +e
"$QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$ELF" -plugin "$PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE" 2>/dev/null
rc=$?
set -e
if [[ $rc -ne 200 ]]; then
  echo "FAIL: QEMU expected exit 200 on drain, got rc=$rc"
  exit 1
fi

# Read 8-byte little-endian uint64 at a byte offset from the state file.
read_u64_le() {
  local off=$1
  od -A n -j "$off" -N 8 -t u8 "$STATE" | tr -d ' \n'
}

got_vxsat=$(read_u64_le 3368)
got_vxrm=$(read_u64_le  3376)
got_vcsr=$(read_u64_le  3384)

echo "vxsat=$got_vxsat (want 1)"
echo "vxrm=$got_vxrm  (want 1)"
echo "vcsr=$got_vcsr   (want 3)"

fail=0
[[ "$got_vxsat" == "1" ]] || { echo "FAIL: vxsat=$got_vxsat != 1"; fail=1; }
[[ "$got_vxrm"  == "1" ]] || { echo "FAIL: vxrm=$got_vxrm != 1";  fail=1; }
[[ "$got_vcsr"  == "3" ]] || { echo "FAIL: vcsr=$got_vcsr != 3";   fail=1; }

if [[ $fail -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "PASS"
  exit 0
else
  exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Negative test: a corrupted --resume-from-qemu state file must trigger a
# fail-fast in vsim with a recognizable error message, not a segfault, hang,
# or silent forward run on garbage state.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
hybrid_resolve_paths

BAD_STATE=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$BAD_STATE" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

# 32 bytes of zeros: shorter than hybrid_state_v1, hits the short-read branch.
dd if=/dev/zero of="$BAD_STATE" bs=32 count=1 status=none

set +e
timeout 10 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$BAD_STATE" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

# 0    - silent pass on garbage (BAD)
# 124  - bash timeout fired -> vsim hung (BAD)
# 200  - hybrid handoff exit (BAD: drain ran, state was treated as valid)
# else - any non-zero error path (OK)
if [ "$RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$RC" -eq 200 ] || [ "$RC" -eq 124 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_corrupt_state]: expected non-zero, non-200, non-timeout exit; got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if ! grep -Eq "short read|bad magic|cannot open" "$SIM_LOG"; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_corrupt_state]: stderr did not match 'short read|bad magic|cannot open'"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[rt_corrupt_state] PASS: vsim fail-fast with rc=$RC"

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
3	# CTest gate. Drives verilator/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
4	# artifacts in this workspace.
5	#
6	# Env overrides:
7	#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
8	#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
9	#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under verilator/build/)
10	#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches
11	#   MODE         csr | pc | both  (default: both) - drives TRIGGER_MODE
12	#                for round-trip fixtures. Negative drivers run once
13	#                regardless because they assert orchestrator failure
14	#                modes orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
15	#   JOBS         parallel job slots (default: min($(nproc),
16	#                free(/dev/shm)/256MB)). Each Tier-2 slot uses a 128 MB
17	#                /dev/shm mmap and runs 3 processes (qemu1, vsim, qemu2);
18	#                the headroom check leaves margin for that plus QEMU's
19	#                own scratch. Pass JOBS=1 for sequential semantics.
20	#   LOG_DIR      where per-test logs land (default: mktemp -d under /tmp)
21	
22	set -euo pipefail
23	
24	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
25	VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/verilator"
26	HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
27	TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"
28	
29	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
30	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
31	if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
32	  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
33	fi
34	
35	step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
36	ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
37	warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
38	fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }
39	
40	require() {
41	  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
42	    fail "$1 not found: $2"
43	    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
44	    exit 1
45	  fi
46	}
47	require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
48	require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
49	require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"
50	
51	export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN
52	
53	REQUIRED_ELFS=(
54	  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
55	  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
56	  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs
57	  rt_c_v_matmul
58	  # icount_spin: CSR-less spin loop. Used by switching-mode demos
59	  # T3/T4/T5/T7 (icount, BBV emit, slice-consume, SimPoint loop).
60	  icount_spin
61	)
62	need_build=0
63	for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
64	  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
65	done
66	if (( need_build )); then
67	  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
68	  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
69	fi
70	
71	# Round-trip fixtures: name|fixture-env-prefix. All run on the unified
72	# e2e.sh harness (shared /dev/shm mmap). The TRIGGER_MODE env is
73	# injected per iteration based on $MODE. Negative drivers live in
74	# NEG_TESTS and run once regardless of mode.
75	RT_TESTS=(
76	  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3"
77	  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump"
78	  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits"
79	  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases"
80	  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi"
81	  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs"
82	  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore"
83	  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh"
84	  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival"
85	  "rt_shared_mem|FIXTURE=rt_shared_mem"
86	  "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello"
87	  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf"
88	  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul TIMEOUT=120"
89	  "rt_c_v_regs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs"
90	  "rt_c_v_csrs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_csrs"
91	  "rt_c_v_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_matmul TIMEOUT=120"
92	  "rt_c_static_buf|FIXTURE=rt_c_static_buf"
93	)
94	
95	NEG_TESTS=(
96	  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
97	  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
98	  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
99	  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/rt_pty_handback.sh"
100	)
101	
102	# Switching-mode demos (T1-T7 in TEST_ROADMAP.md). Each demo exercises
103	# one of the M1-M8 trigger pathways end-to-end. Run once (no
104	# csr/pc/both loop) because each script hard-codes its trigger choice.
105	DEMO_TESTS=(
106	  "demo_csr_oneshot|bash $HARNESS/demo_csr_oneshot.sh"
107	  "demo_pc_oneshot|bash $HARNESS/demo_pc_oneshot.sh"
108	  "demo_icount_roundtrip|bash $HARNESS/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh"
109	  "demo_bbv_profile|bash $HARNESS/demo_bbv_profile.sh"
110	  "demo_slice_consume_roundtrip|bash $HARNESS/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh"
111	  "demo_archive_replay|bash $HARNESS/demo_archive_replay.sh"
112	  "demo_simpoint_loop|bash $HARNESS/demo_simpoint_loop.sh"
113	)
114	
115	MODE="${MODE:-both}"
116	case "$MODE" in
117	  csr|pc|both) ;;
118	  *) fail "MODE must be one of csr|pc|both (got: $MODE)"; exit 1 ;;
119	esac
120	
121	default_jobs() {
122	  local n=1 shm_free shm_cap
123	  n=$(nproc 2>/dev/null || echo 1)
124	  if [[ -d /dev/shm ]]; then
125	    shm_free=$(df -m --output=avail /dev/shm 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | tr -d ' ')
126	    if [[ "$shm_free" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$shm_free" -gt 0 ]]; then
127	      shm_cap=$((shm_free / 256))
128	      (( shm_cap < n && shm_cap >= 1 )) && n=$shm_cap
129	    fi
130	  fi
131	  (( n < 1 )) && n=1
132	  printf '%d\n' "$n"
133	}
134	JOBS="${JOBS:-$(default_jobs)}"
135	[[ "$JOBS" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$JOBS" -ge 1 ]] \
136	  || { fail "JOBS must be a positive integer (got: $JOBS)"; exit 1; }
137	
138	FILTER="${FILTER:-}"
139	
140	LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-$(mktemp -d -t e2e-XXXXXX)}"
141	mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
142	RESULTS_FILE="$LOG_DIR/results.tsv"
143	LOCK_FILE="$LOG_DIR/lock"
144	: > "$RESULTS_FILE"
145	: > "$LOCK_FILE"
146	
147	# run_one MODE NAME CMD INDEX TOTAL
148	# Captures the test command's stdout+stderr into $LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log,
149	# emits one atomic progress line, and appends a TSV row to $RESULTS_FILE
150	# (mode<TAB>name<TAB>status<TAB>rc<TAB>elapsed). flock on $LOCK_FILE
151	# serializes the progress write and the results-file append so concurrent
152	# children (introduced by the parallel-execution commit) cannot interleave
153	# their TTY writes or corrupt the tally file.
154	run_one() {
155	  local mode="$1" name="$2" cmd="$3" idx="$4" total="$5"
156	  local log="$LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log"
157	  local start end elapsed status rc
158	  start=$(date +%s)
159	  set +e
160	  bash -c "$cmd" >"$log" 2>&1
161	  rc=$?
162	  set -e
163	  end=$(date +%s)
164	  elapsed=$((end - start))
165	  case $rc in
166	    0)  status=PASS ;;
167	    77) status=SKIP ;;
168	    *)  status=FAIL ;;
169	  esac
170	  (
171	    flock 9
172	    case "$status" in
173	      PASS) ok   "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (${elapsed}s)" ;;
174	      SKIP) warn "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=77, ${elapsed}s)" ;;
175	      FAIL) fail "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=$rc, ${elapsed}s) -> $log" ;;
176	    esac
177	    printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%d\t%d\n' \
178	      "$mode" "$name" "$status" "$rc" "$elapsed" >> "$RESULTS_FILE"
179	  ) 9>"$LOCK_FILE"
180	}
181	
182	# Build the workplan: one entry per (mode, name, cmd) tuple. Order
183	# matters: csr-mode tests first, then pc-mode, then negatives (matching
184	# the original sequential layout so logs are streamed in the same order).
185	declare -a WORKPLAN=()
186	for tm in csr pc; do
187	  [[ "$MODE" != "both" && "$MODE" != "$tm" ]] && continue
188	  for entry in "${RT_TESTS[@]}"; do
189	    rt_name="${entry%%|*}"
190	    env_prefix="${entry#*|}"
191	    [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$rt_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
192	    cmd="TRIGGER_MODE=$tm $env_prefix bash $HARNESS/e2e.sh"
193	    WORKPLAN+=("$tm|$rt_name|$cmd")
194	  done
195	done
196	for entry in "${NEG_TESTS[@]}"; do
197	  neg_name="${entry%%|*}"
198	  neg_cmd="${entry#*|}"
199	  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$neg_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
200	  WORKPLAN+=("neg|$neg_name|$neg_cmd")
201	done
202	for entry in "${DEMO_TESTS[@]}"; do
203	  demo_name="${entry%%|*}"
204	  demo_cmd="${entry#*|}"
205	  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$demo_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
206	  WORKPLAN+=("demo|$demo_name|$demo_cmd")
207	done
208	
209	TOTAL=${#WORKPLAN[@]}
210	step "logs: $LOG_DIR  jobs: $JOBS  mode: $MODE  tests: $TOTAL"
211	
212	# FIFO-based bounded job pool. Each child reads one token from $SEM_FIFO
213	# before starting and writes one back on exit; only $JOBS tokens exist,
214	# so at most $JOBS children run concurrently. Opening the fifo in
215	# read-write mode (<>) means writes never block the parent. The fifo
216	# inode is unlinked immediately after fd 8 is bound -- the kernel keeps
217	# the pipe alive as long as fd 8 is open.
218	SEM_FIFO="$LOG_DIR/sem.fifo"
219	mkfifo "$SEM_FIFO"
220	exec 8<>"$SEM_FIFO"
221	rm -f "$SEM_FIFO"
222	for ((sem_i=0; sem_i<JOBS; sem_i++)); do
223	  printf '.\n' >&8
224	done
225	
226	i=0
227	for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
228	  i=$((i+1))
229	  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
230	  w_rest="${w#*|}"
231	  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
232	  w_cmd="${w_rest#*|}"
233	  read -r -u 8 _tok
234	  (
235	    run_one "$w_mode" "$w_name" "$w_cmd" "$i" "$TOTAL"
236	    printf '.\n' >&8
237	  ) &
238	done
239	wait
240	exec 8>&-
241	
242	declare -A MODE_PASS=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0 [demo]=0)
243	declare -A MODE_FAIL=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0 [demo]=0)
244	declare -A MODE_SKIP=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0 [demo]=0)
245	declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()
246	while IFS=$'\t' read -r r_mode r_name r_status _r_rc _r_el; do
247	  case "$r_status" in
248	    PASS) MODE_PASS[$r_mode]=$((MODE_PASS[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
249	    SKIP) MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]=$((MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
250	    FAIL) MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]=$((MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]+1));
251	          FAILED_NAMES+=("${r_name}[${r_mode}]") ;;
252	  esac
253	done < "$RESULTS_FILE"
254	
255	# Stream failed-test logs in workplan order so the user sees a
256	# deterministic post-run failure dump.
257	for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
258	  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
259	  w_rest="${w#*|}"
260	  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
261	  w_status=$(awk -F'\t' -v m="$w_mode" -v n="$w_name" \
262	                 '$1==m&&$2==n{print $3; exit}' "$RESULTS_FILE")
263	  if [[ "$w_status" == "FAIL" ]]; then
264	    echo
265	    echo "=== FAIL log: ${w_name}[${w_mode}] ==="
266	    cat "$LOG_DIR/$w_mode--$w_name.log"
267	  fi
268	done
269	
270	echo
271	csr_p=${MODE_PASS[csr]} csr_f=${MODE_FAIL[csr]} csr_s=${MODE_SKIP[csr]}
272	pc_p=${MODE_PASS[pc]}   pc_f=${MODE_FAIL[pc]}   pc_s=${MODE_SKIP[pc]}
273	neg_p=${MODE_PASS[neg]} neg_f=${MODE_FAIL[neg]} neg_s=${MODE_SKIP[neg]}
274	dem_p=${MODE_PASS[demo]} dem_f=${MODE_FAIL[demo]} dem_s=${MODE_SKIP[demo]}
275	total_pass=$((csr_p + pc_p + neg_p + dem_p))
276	total_fail=$((csr_f + pc_f + neg_f + dem_f))
277	total_skip=$((csr_s + pc_s + neg_s + dem_s))
278	step "summary: csr ${csr_p}p/${csr_f}f/${csr_s}s  pc ${pc_p}p/${pc_f}f/${pc_s}s  neg ${neg_p}p/${neg_f}f/${neg_s}s  demo ${dem_p}p/${dem_f}f/${dem_s}s  total ${total_pass} passed, ${total_fail} failed, ${total_skip} skipped"
279	if (( total_fail > 0 )); then
280	  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
281	  exit 1
282	fi
283

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives verilator/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under verilator/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches
#   MODE         csr | pc | both  (default: both) - drives TRIGGER_MODE
#                for round-trip fixtures. Negative drivers run once
#                regardless because they assert orchestrator failure
#                modes orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
#   JOBS         parallel job slots (default: min($(nproc),
#                free(/dev/shm)/256MB)). Each Tier-2 slot uses a 128 MB
#                /dev/shm mmap and runs 3 processes (qemu1, vsim, qemu2);
#                the headroom check leaves margin for that plus QEMU's
#                own scratch. Pass JOBS=1 for sequential semantics.
#   LOG_DIR      where per-test logs land (default: mktemp -d under /tmp)

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/verilator"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs
  rt_c_v_matmul
  # icount_spin: CSR-less spin loop. Used by switching-mode demos
  # T3/T4/T5/T7 (icount, BBV emit, slice-consume, SimPoint loop).
  icount_spin
)
need_build=0
for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
done
if (( need_build )); then
  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
fi

# Round-trip fixtures: name|fixture-env-prefix. All run on the unified
# e2e.sh harness (shared /dev/shm mmap). The TRIGGER_MODE env is
# injected per iteration based on $MODE. Negative drivers live in
# NEG_TESTS and run once regardless of mode.
RT_TESTS=(
  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3"
  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump"
  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits"
  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases"
  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi"
  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs"
  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore"
  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh"
  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival"
  "rt_shared_mem|FIXTURE=rt_shared_mem"
  "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello"
  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf"
  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul TIMEOUT=120"
  "rt_c_v_regs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs"
  "rt_c_v_csrs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_csrs"
  "rt_c_v_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_matmul TIMEOUT=120"
  "rt_c_static_buf|FIXTURE=rt_c_static_buf"
)

NEG_TESTS=(
  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/rt_pty_handback.sh"
)

# Switching-mode demos (T1-T7 in TEST_ROADMAP.md). Each demo exercises
# one of the M1-M8 trigger pathways end-to-end. Run once (no
# csr/pc/both loop) because each script hard-codes its trigger choice.
DEMO_TESTS=(
  "demo_csr_oneshot|bash $HARNESS/demo_csr_oneshot.sh"
  "demo_pc_oneshot|bash $HARNESS/demo_pc_oneshot.sh"
  "demo_icount_roundtrip|bash $HARNESS/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh"
  "demo_bbv_profile|bash $HARNESS/demo_bbv_profile.sh"
  "demo_slice_consume_roundtrip|bash $HARNESS/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh"
  "demo_archive_replay|bash $HARNESS/demo_archive_replay.sh"
  "demo_simpoint_loop|bash $HARNESS/demo_simpoint_loop.sh"
)

MODE="${MODE:-both}"
case "$MODE" in
  csr|pc|both) ;;
  *) fail "MODE must be one of csr|pc|both (got: $MODE)"; exit 1 ;;
esac

default_jobs() {
  local n=1 shm_free shm_cap
  n=$(nproc 2>/dev/null || echo 1)
  if [[ -d /dev/shm ]]; then
    shm_free=$(df -m --output=avail /dev/shm 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | tr -d ' ')
    if [[ "$shm_free" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$shm_free" -gt 0 ]]; then
      shm_cap=$((shm_free / 256))
      (( shm_cap < n && shm_cap >= 1 )) && n=$shm_cap
    fi
  fi
  (( n < 1 )) && n=1
  printf '%d\n' "$n"
}
JOBS="${JOBS:-$(default_jobs)}"
[[ "$JOBS" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$JOBS" -ge 1 ]] \
  || { fail "JOBS must be a positive integer (got: $JOBS)"; exit 1; }

FILTER="${FILTER:-}"

LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-$(mktemp -d -t e2e-XXXXXX)}"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
RESULTS_FILE="$LOG_DIR/results.tsv"
LOCK_FILE="$LOG_DIR/lock"
: > "$RESULTS_FILE"
: > "$LOCK_FILE"

# run_one MODE NAME CMD INDEX TOTAL
# Captures the test command's stdout+stderr into $LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log,
# emits one atomic progress line, and appends a TSV row to $RESULTS_FILE
# (mode<TAB>name<TAB>status<TAB>rc<TAB>elapsed). flock on $LOCK_FILE
# serializes the progress write and the results-file append so concurrent
# children (introduced by the parallel-execution commit) cannot interleave
# their TTY writes or corrupt the tally file.
run_one() {
  local mode="$1" name="$2" cmd="$3" idx="$4" total="$5"
  local log="$LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log"
  local start end elapsed status rc
  start=$(date +%s)
  set +e
  bash -c "$cmd" >"$log" 2>&1
  rc=$?
  set -e
  end=$(date +%s)
  elapsed=$((end - start))
  case $rc in
    0)  status=PASS ;;
    77) status=SKIP ;;
    *)  status=FAIL ;;
  esac
  (
    flock 9
    case "$status" in
      PASS) ok   "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (${elapsed}s)" ;;
      SKIP) warn "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=77, ${elapsed}s)" ;;
      FAIL) fail "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=$rc, ${elapsed}s) -> $log" ;;
    esac
    printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%d\t%d\n' \
      "$mode" "$name" "$status" "$rc" "$elapsed" >> "$RESULTS_FILE"
  ) 9>"$LOCK_FILE"
}

# Build the workplan: one entry per (mode, name, cmd) tuple. Order
# matters: csr-mode tests first, then pc-mode, then negatives (matching
# the original sequential layout so logs are streamed in the same order).
declare -a WORKPLAN=()
for tm in csr pc; do
  [[ "$MODE" != "both" && "$MODE" != "$tm" ]] && continue
  for entry in "${RT_TESTS[@]}"; do
    rt_name="${entry%%|*}"
    env_prefix="${entry#*|}"
    [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$rt_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
    cmd="TRIGGER_MODE=$tm $env_prefix bash $HARNESS/e2e.sh"
    WORKPLAN+=("$tm|$rt_name|$cmd")
  done
done
for entry in "${NEG_TESTS[@]}"; do
  neg_name="${entry%%|*}"
  neg_cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$neg_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  WORKPLAN+=("neg|$neg_name|$neg_cmd")
done
for entry in "${DEMO_TESTS[@]}"; do
  demo_name="${entry%%|*}"
  demo_cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$demo_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  WORKPLAN+=("demo|$demo_name|$demo_cmd")
done

TOTAL=${#WORKPLAN[@]}
step "logs: $LOG_DIR  jobs: $JOBS  mode: $MODE  tests: $TOTAL"

# FIFO-based bounded job pool. Each child reads one token from $SEM_FIFO
# before starting and writes one back on exit; only $JOBS tokens exist,
# so at most $JOBS children run concurrently. Opening the fifo in
# read-write mode (<>) means writes never block the parent. The fifo
# inode is unlinked immediately after fd 8 is bound -- the kernel keeps
# the pipe alive as long as fd 8 is open.
SEM_FIFO="$LOG_DIR/sem.fifo"
mkfifo "$SEM_FIFO"
exec 8<>"$SEM_FIFO"
rm -f "$SEM_FIFO"
for ((sem_i=0; sem_i<JOBS; sem_i++)); do
  printf '.\n' >&8
done

i=0
for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
  i=$((i+1))
  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
  w_rest="${w#*|}"
  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
  w_cmd="${w_rest#*|}"
  read -r -u 8 _tok
  (
    run_one "$w_mode" "$w_name" "$w_cmd" "$i" "$TOTAL"
    printf '.\n' >&8
  ) &
done
wait
exec 8>&-

declare -A MODE_PASS=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0 [demo]=0)
declare -A MODE_FAIL=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0 [demo]=0)
declare -A MODE_SKIP=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0 [demo]=0)
declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()
while IFS=$'\t' read -r r_mode r_name r_status _r_rc _r_el; do
  case "$r_status" in
    PASS) MODE_PASS[$r_mode]=$((MODE_PASS[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
    SKIP) MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]=$((MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
    FAIL) MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]=$((MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]+1));
          FAILED_NAMES+=("${r_name}[${r_mode}]") ;;
  esac
done < "$RESULTS_FILE"

# Stream failed-test logs in workplan order so the user sees a
# deterministic post-run failure dump.
for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
  w_rest="${w#*|}"
  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
  w_status=$(awk -F'\t' -v m="$w_mode" -v n="$w_name" \
                 '$1==m&&$2==n{print $3; exit}' "$RESULTS_FILE")
  if [[ "$w_status" == "FAIL" ]]; then
    echo
    echo "=== FAIL log: ${w_name}[${w_mode}] ==="
    cat "$LOG_DIR/$w_mode--$w_name.log"
  fi
done

echo
csr_p=${MODE_PASS[csr]} csr_f=${MODE_FAIL[csr]} csr_s=${MODE_SKIP[csr]}
pc_p=${MODE_PASS[pc]}   pc_f=${MODE_FAIL[pc]}   pc_s=${MODE_SKIP[pc]}
neg_p=${MODE_PASS[neg]} neg_f=${MODE_FAIL[neg]} neg_s=${MODE_SKIP[neg]}
dem_p=${MODE_PASS[demo]} dem_f=${MODE_FAIL[demo]} dem_s=${MODE_SKIP[demo]}
total_pass=$((csr_p + pc_p + neg_p + dem_p))
total_fail=$((csr_f + pc_f + neg_f + dem_f))
total_skip=$((csr_s + pc_s + neg_s + dem_s))
step "summary: csr ${csr_p}p/${csr_f}f/${csr_s}s  pc ${pc_p}p/${pc_f}f/${pc_s}s  neg ${neg_p}p/${neg_f}f/${neg_s}s  demo ${dem_p}p/${dem_f}f/${dem_s}s  total ${total_pass} passed, ${total_fail} failed, ${total_skip} skipped"
if (( total_fail > 0 )); then
  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
  exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# F1 contract: QEMU plugin exits with code 200 on drain (matches vsim's
# kExitHandoffToQemu). 0 is reserved for "workload completed normally".
# The orchestrator (F2+) uses this distinction to decide the next spawn.
#
# Required environment (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF     a CSR-trigger fixture (default: handoff_roundtrip.elf)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$QEMU_LOG"' EXIT

set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_drain_exit_code]: expected QEMU exit=200 on drain, got $RC"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_drain_exit_code]: state file is empty"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[plugin_drain_exit_code] PASS: QEMU exit=$RC, state size=$(wc -c <"$STATE_QEMU") bytes"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# F5 contract: QEMU plugin icount-trigger mode. With plugin arg `icount=N`
# the plugin drains and exits 200 after N retired insns - even on a binary
# that never executes the magic CSR. Foundation for SimPoint slice-consume
# (F11) and the orchestrator's icount-based switching (F8).
#
# Test binary: icount_spin.elf (no csrwi 0x7C0; linked at 0x80000000 for
# andes_ae350). Today's plugin would run forever; the new icount path
# must fire the drain.
#
# Required environment (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF     a CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/icount_spin.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

ICOUNT_TARGET=100000
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-icount-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$QEMU_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU,icount=$ICOUNT_TARGET" \
  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_icount_trigger]: expected QEMU exit=200 on drain, got $RC"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_icount_trigger]: state file is empty"
  exit 1
fi

# minstret lives at offset 632 (state_abi_check.h:35), little-endian uint64.
MINSTRET=$(python3 -c "
import struct, sys
with open('$STATE_QEMU', 'rb') as f:
    f.seek(632)
    print(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0])
")

if [ "$MINSTRET" -lt "$ICOUNT_TARGET" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_icount_trigger]: minstret=$MINSTRET < icount=$ICOUNT_TARGET"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[plugin_icount_trigger] PASS: QEMU exit=$RC, minstret=$MINSTRET (target=$ICOUNT_TARGET)"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# F11 contract: QEMU plugin BBV slice-consume mode. With plugin args
# slice=N,slice_at=K the plugin counts slice boundaries (one per N
# retired insns) and drains at the end of slice index K (0-based). For
# K=2, drain fires at insn 3*N -- the boundary that closes the third
# slice. Used by the orchestrator (F8) to chain spawn invocations and
# produce one snapshot per SimPoint cluster representative slice.
#
# Test binary: icount_spin.elf (CSR-less, predictable tight loop).
# Plugin must:
#   1. Accept slice= and slice_at= args.
#   2. Drain at insn (slice_at+1)*slice (the END of slice index
#      slice_at, 0-based).
#   3. Exit 200 (drain) before any icount fallback fires.
#
# Required environment (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF     CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/icount_spin.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

SLICE=1000
SLICE_AT=2                                # 0-based: third slice
ICOUNT_FALLBACK=10000                     # safety upper bound
EXPECTED_MIN=$(( (SLICE_AT + 1) * SLICE )) # = 3000

SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-phase-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_OUT=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_OUT" "$QEMU_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_OUT,slice=$SLICE,slice_at=$SLICE_AT,icount=$ICOUNT_FALLBACK" \
  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: expected QEMU exit=200, got $RC"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_OUT" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: state file is empty"
  exit 1
fi

# Verify drain timing via the plugin's own slice counter, not minstret
# (which is a cycle-counter proxy on andes-ax45mpv). At drain the plugin
# logs `idx=N target=K`; for slice_at=K, N must be K+1 (slices fully
# retired). slice_at=2 -> idx=3.
EXPECTED_IDX=$((SLICE_AT + 1))
if ! grep -qE "slice-consume drain \(idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT\)" "$QEMU_LOG"; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: did not find drain at idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

# Sanity: drained PC should be inside icount_spin's tight loop body
# (0x100004 addi, 0x100008 j). Anything outside means drain fired in the
# wrong place. pc lives at state-file offset 16 (state_abi_check.h:18).
PC=$(python3 -c "
import struct
with open('$STATE_OUT', 'rb') as f:
    f.seek(16)
    print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0]))
")
if [ "$PC" != "0x100004" ] && [ "$PC" != "0x100008" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: drained pc=$PC not in icount_spin loop body"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[plugin_phase_consume] PASS: drain idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT pc=$PC"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
