toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T7: full SimPoint pipeline (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7).
#
# Centerpiece demo showing every switching mode in the production
# SimPoint flow:
#
#   M4 - profile pass: QEMU+plugin bbv_out=,slice=N -> .bb file
#   ---  cluster      : tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint -loadFVFile ...
#   M5 - per-phase    : QEMU+plugin slice=N,slice_at=K,outfile= -> drain
#   M6 - archive      : write snap/K/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}
#   M7 - replay       : orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim
#
# Workload: rt_c_v_regs (C-level Tier-2 fixture with natural csrwi
# 0x7C0,1 exit marker, so replay's hardcoded vsim+csr trigger drains
# naturally without --hybrid-icount tuning).
#
# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
#   VSIM_ELF      Tier-2 fixture (default: rt_c_v_regs.elf)
#   SIMPOINT_BIN  tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint
#   WORKDIR       persistent dir (default: mktemp -d)
#   SLICE         insns per BBV row / per slice (default: 200)
#   PROFILE_ICOUNT plugin icount fallback for profile pass (default: 8000)
#   PHASE_ICOUNT  plugin icount fallback per phase run (default: 8000)
#   MAXK          simpoint -maxK (default: 2)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_c_v_regs}"
SIMPOINT_BIN="${SIMPOINT_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint}"
WORKDIR="${WORKDIR:-$(mktemp -d -t demo-simpoint-XXXXXX)}"
SLICE="${SLICE:-20}"
PROFILE_ICOUNT="${PROFILE_ICOUNT:-2000}"
PHASE_ICOUNT="${PHASE_ICOUNT:-2000}"
MAXK="${MAXK:-2}"
# Defaults are tuned for rt_c_v_regs which reaches csrwi 0x7C0,0 after
# ~200-400 insns of QEMU1 startup. slice=20 gives ~15 BBV rows, enough
# for simpoint to find 2 clusters. Tune via env for other fixtures.

MODE_LABEL="Full SimPoint loop (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Workload: $(basename "$VSIM_ELF")"
echo "Slice: $SLICE insns; profile icount: $PROFILE_ICOUNT; maxK: $MAXK"
echo "Workdir: $WORKDIR"
echo "=========================================================="

hybrid_resolve_paths
if [ ! -x "$SIMPOINT_BIN" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: $SIMPOINT_BIN not built"; exit 77
fi
if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: python3 not found"; exit 77
fi

mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
BBV="$WORKDIR/profile.bb"
SIMPTS="$WORKDIR/simpts"
WEIGHTS="$WORKDIR/weights"
SP_LOG="$WORKDIR/simpoint.log"
SHM_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))

# ---- M4: profile pass ----------------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 1/4] M4 profile: bbv_out=,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT"
SHM_PROFILE=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-t7-profile.XXXXXX)
PROFILE_STATE="$WORKDIR/profile_state.bin"
PROFILE_LOG="$WORKDIR/profile_qemu.log"
truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM_PROFILE"
trap 'rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"' EXIT

set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM_PROFILE,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$PROFILE_STATE,bbv_out=$BBV,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT" \
  >"$PROFILE_LOG" 2>&1
PRC=$?
set -e
rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"

if [ "$PRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: profile QEMU expected exit 200, got $PRC"
  tail -20 "$PROFILE_LOG"; exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$BBV" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: BBV file empty"; exit 1
fi
BBV_ROWS=$(wc -l < "$BBV")
echo "  $BBV_ROWS BBV rows -> $BBV"

# ---- M4-cluster: simpoint -----------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 2/4] cluster: simpoint -maxK $MAXK -dim 2"
set +e
"$SIMPOINT_BIN" -loadFVFile "$BBV" -maxK "$MAXK" -dim 2 \
  -saveSimpoints "$SIMPTS" -saveSimpointWeights "$WEIGHTS" \
  >"$SP_LOG" 2>&1
SP_RC=$?
set -e
if [ "$SP_RC" -ne 0 ] || [ ! -s "$SIMPTS" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: simpoint rc=$SP_RC; simpts size=$(wc -c <"$SIMPTS" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  tail -20 "$SP_LOG"; exit 1
fi
NUM_PHASES=$(wc -l <"$SIMPTS")
echo "  $NUM_PHASES cluster representatives:"
awk '{printf "    slice=%s cluster=%s\n", $1, $2}' "$SIMPTS"

# ---- M5+M6: per-phase drain + archive -----------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 3/4] for each phase: drain at slice_at=K + archive"
PHASE_INDICES=$(awk '{print $1}' "$SIMPTS")
declare -a ARCHIVES=()
for K in $PHASE_INDICES; do
  SHM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm "vsim-t7-phase${K}.XXXXXX")
  truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM"
  STATE="$WORKDIR/phase_${K}_state.bin"
  Q_LOG="$WORKDIR/phase_${K}_qemu.log"

  set +e
  timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
    -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
    -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
    -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
    -nographic \
    -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
    -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
    -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE,slice=$SLICE,slice_at=$K,icount=$PHASE_ICOUNT" \
    >"$Q_LOG" 2>&1
  QRC=$?
  set -e

  if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
    rm -f "$SHM"
    echo "[demo T7] FAIL: phase $K QEMU exit $QRC"; tail -20 "$Q_LOG"; exit 1
  fi

  # M6: write snap/<K>/{state.bin, mem, meta.json} via the orchestrator's
  # write_archive helper (the canonical M6 entry point used by F9).
  SNAP="$WORKDIR/snap/$K"
  set +e
  PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" python3 - <<PY
from pathlib import Path
from orchestrator.checkpoint import write_archive
write_archive(
    Path("$SNAP"),
    state_path=Path("$STATE"),
    mmap_path=Path("$SHM"),
    mmap_size=$SHM_SIZE,
    elf_path="tests/fixtures/$(basename "$VSIM_ELF")",
    source_step_index=0,
    cpu_variant="ax45mpv_premium",
    region_id=f"snap/$K",
    slice_index=$K,
)
PY
  AR_RC=$?
  set -e
  rm -f "$SHM"
  if [ "$AR_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "[demo T7] FAIL: write_archive for phase $K (rc=$AR_RC)"; exit 1
  fi
  for f in state.bin mem meta.json; do
    if [ ! -s "$SNAP/$f" ]; then
      echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $SNAP/$f missing/empty"; exit 1
    fi
  done
  ARCHIVES+=("$SNAP")
  echo "  phase $K archived -> $SNAP"
done

# ---- M7: replay each archive --------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 4/4] for each archive: replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim"
REPLAY_OK=0
for SNAP in "${ARCHIVES[@]}"; do
  K=$(basename "$SNAP")
  REPLAY_WD="$WORKDIR/replay_$K"
  R_LOG="$WORKDIR/replay_${K}.log"
  mkdir -p "$REPLAY_WD"
  set +e
  PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" VSIM_QEMU="$VSIM_QEMU" \
    VSIM_PLUGIN="$VSIM_PLUGIN" VSIM_BIN="$VSIM_BIN" \
    timeout 120 python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint "$SNAP" \
      --resume-with vsim --project-root "$TOP_ROOT" --workdir "$REPLAY_WD" \
      >"$R_LOG" 2>&1
  R_RC=$?
  set -e
  if [ "$R_RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$R_RC" -eq 200 ]; then
    echo "  phase $K: replay OK (rc=$R_RC)"
    REPLAY_OK=$((REPLAY_OK + 1))
  else
    echo "  phase $K: replay FAILED (rc=$R_RC)"
    tail -15 "$R_LOG"
  fi
done

if [ "$REPLAY_OK" -ne "$NUM_PHASES" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $REPLAY_OK/$NUM_PHASES replays succeeded"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[demo T7] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): bbv_rows=$BBV_ROWS phases=$NUM_PHASES replays_ok=$REPLAY_OK workdir=$WORKDIR"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T7: full SimPoint pipeline (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7).
#
# Centerpiece demo showing every switching mode in the production
# SimPoint flow:
#
#   M4 - profile pass: QEMU+plugin bbv_out=,slice=N -> .bb file
#   ---  cluster      : tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint -loadFVFile ...
#   M5 - per-phase    : QEMU+plugin slice=N,slice_at=K,outfile= -> drain
#   M6 - archive      : write snap/K/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}
#   M7 - replay       : orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim
#
# Workload: rt_c_v_regs (C-level Tier-2 fixture with natural csrwi
# 0x7C0,1 exit marker, so replay's hardcoded vsim+csr trigger drains
# naturally without --hybrid-icount tuning).
#
# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
#   VSIM_ELF      Tier-2 fixture (default: rt_c_v_regs.elf)
#   SIMPOINT_BIN  tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint
#   WORKDIR       persistent dir (default: mktemp -d)
#   SLICE         insns per BBV row / per slice (default: 200)
#   PROFILE_ICOUNT plugin icount fallback for profile pass (default: 8000)
#   PHASE_ICOUNT  plugin icount fallback per phase run (default: 8000)
#   MAXK          simpoint -maxK (default: 2)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_c_v_regs}"
SIMPOINT_BIN="${SIMPOINT_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint}"
WORKDIR="${WORKDIR:-$(mktemp -d -t demo-simpoint-XXXXXX)}"
SLICE="${SLICE:-20}"
PROFILE_ICOUNT="${PROFILE_ICOUNT:-2000}"
PHASE_ICOUNT="${PHASE_ICOUNT:-2000}"
MAXK="${MAXK:-2}"
# Defaults are tuned for rt_c_v_regs which reaches csrwi 0x7C0,0 after
# ~200-400 insns of QEMU1 startup. slice=20 gives ~15 BBV rows, enough
# for simpoint to find 2 clusters. Tune via env for other fixtures.

MODE_LABEL="Full SimPoint loop (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Workload: $(basename "$VSIM_ELF")"
echo "Slice: $SLICE insns; profile icount: $PROFILE_ICOUNT; maxK: $MAXK"
echo "Workdir: $WORKDIR"
echo "=========================================================="

if [ ! -x "$SIMPOINT_BIN" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: $SIMPOINT_BIN not built"; exit 77
fi
if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: python3 not found"; exit 77
fi

mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
BBV="$WORKDIR/profile.bb"
SIMPTS="$WORKDIR/simpts"
WEIGHTS="$WORKDIR/weights"
SP_LOG="$WORKDIR/simpoint.log"
SHM_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))

# ---- M4: profile pass ----------------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 1/4] M4 profile: bbv_out=,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT"
SHM_PROFILE=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-t7-profile.XXXXXX)
PROFILE_STATE="$WORKDIR/profile_state.bin"
PROFILE_LOG="$WORKDIR/profile_qemu.log"
truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM_PROFILE"
trap 'rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"' EXIT

set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM_PROFILE,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$PROFILE_STATE,bbv_out=$BBV,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT" \
  >"$PROFILE_LOG" 2>&1
PRC=$?
set -e
rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"

if [ "$PRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: profile QEMU expected exit 200, got $PRC"
  tail -20 "$PROFILE_LOG"; exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$BBV" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: BBV file empty"; exit 1
fi
BBV_ROWS=$(wc -l < "$BBV")
echo "  $BBV_ROWS BBV rows -> $BBV"

# ---- M4-cluster: simpoint -----------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 2/4] cluster: simpoint -maxK $MAXK -dim 2"
set +e
"$SIMPOINT_BIN" -loadFVFile "$BBV" -maxK "$MAXK" -dim 2 \
  -saveSimpoints "$SIMPTS" -saveSimpointWeights "$WEIGHTS" \
  >"$SP_LOG" 2>&1
SP_RC=$?
set -e
if [ "$SP_RC" -ne 0 ] || [ ! -s "$SIMPTS" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: simpoint rc=$SP_RC; simpts size=$(wc -c <"$SIMPTS" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  tail -20 "$SP_LOG"; exit 1
fi
NUM_PHASES=$(wc -l <"$SIMPTS")
echo "  $NUM_PHASES cluster representatives:"
awk '{printf "    slice=%s cluster=%s\n", $1, $2}' "$SIMPTS"

# ---- M5+M6: per-phase drain + archive -----------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 3/4] for each phase: drain at slice_at=K + archive"
PHASE_INDICES=$(awk '{print $1}' "$SIMPTS")
declare -a ARCHIVES=()
for K in $PHASE_INDICES; do
  SHM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm "vsim-t7-phase${K}.XXXXXX")
  truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM"
  STATE="$WORKDIR/phase_${K}_state.bin"
  Q_LOG="$WORKDIR/phase_${K}_qemu.log"

  set +e
  timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
    -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
    -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
    -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
    -nographic \
    -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
    -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
    -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE,slice=$SLICE,slice_at=$K,icount=$PHASE_ICOUNT" \
    >"$Q_LOG" 2>&1
  QRC=$?
  set -e

  if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
    rm -f "$SHM"
    echo "[demo T7] FAIL: phase $K QEMU exit $QRC"; tail -20 "$Q_LOG"; exit 1
  fi

  # M6: write snap/<K>/{state.bin, mem, meta.json} via the orchestrator's
  # write_archive helper (the canonical M6 entry point used by F9).
  SNAP="$WORKDIR/snap/$K"
  set +e
  PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" python3 - <<PY
from pathlib import Path
from orchestrator.checkpoint import write_archive
write_archive(
    Path("$SNAP"),
    state_path=Path("$STATE"),
    mmap_path=Path("$SHM"),
    mmap_size=$SHM_SIZE,
    elf_path="tests/fixtures/$(basename "$VSIM_ELF")",
    source_step_index=0,
    cpu_variant="ax45mpv_premium",
    region_id=f"snap/$K",
    slice_index=$K,
)
PY
  AR_RC=$?
  set -e
  rm -f "$SHM"
  if [ "$AR_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "[demo T7] FAIL: write_archive for phase $K (rc=$AR_RC)"; exit 1
  fi
  for f in state.bin mem meta.json; do
    if [ ! -s "$SNAP/$f" ]; then
      echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $SNAP/$f missing/empty"; exit 1
    fi
  done
  ARCHIVES+=("$SNAP")
  echo "  phase $K archived -> $SNAP"
done

# ---- M7: replay each archive --------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 4/4] for each archive: replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim"
REPLAY_OK=0
for SNAP in "${ARCHIVES[@]}"; do
  K=$(basename "$SNAP")
  REPLAY_WD="$WORKDIR/replay_$K"
  R_LOG="$WORKDIR/replay_${K}.log"
  mkdir -p "$REPLAY_WD"
  set +e
  PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" VSIM_QEMU="$VSIM_QEMU" \
    VSIM_PLUGIN="$VSIM_PLUGIN" VSIM_BIN="$VSIM_BIN" \
    timeout 120 python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint "$SNAP" \
      --resume-with vsim --project-root "$TOP_ROOT" --workdir "$REPLAY_WD" \
      >"$R_LOG" 2>&1
  R_RC=$?
  set -e
  if [ "$R_RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$R_RC" -eq 200 ]; then
    echo "  phase $K: replay OK (rc=$R_RC)"
    REPLAY_OK=$((REPLAY_OK + 1))
  else
    echo "  phase $K: replay FAILED (rc=$R_RC)"
    tail -15 "$R_LOG"
  fi
done

if [ "$REPLAY_OK" -ne "$NUM_PHASES" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $REPLAY_OK/$NUM_PHASES replays succeeded"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[demo T7] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): bbv_rows=$BBV_ROWS phases=$NUM_PHASES replays_ok=$REPLAY_OK workdir=$WORKDIR"

- Status: in-progress

- Description: The copied `tests/e2e.sh` still computes
  `REPO_ROOT=$SCRIPT_DIR/../..` which assumes it lives inside the submodule,
  so its `VSIM_BIN`/`VSIM_PLUGIN`/`VSIM_ELF` paths are wrong from the new

    because path resolution lands in the wrong directory.
    - Commit: `behavioral: add failing smoke test for top-level tests/e2e.sh`
  - **F2b (GREEN) [done, 48b3fe7]**: Edit `tests/e2e.sh` minimally so `TOP_ROOT=$SCRIPT_DIR/..`
    and the four resolved paths point at top-level locations:
    | Path        | New value                                            |
    |-------------|------------------------------------------------------|
    | `VSIM_BIN`    | `$TOP_ROOT/verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium`    |
    | `VSIM_QEMU`   | `$TOP_ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64`           |

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Negative test: --qemu-binary points at a missing file. Vsim's drain must
3	# still fire and the spawn-fail must propagate as exit 127 (the standard
4	# exec-failure code), not as a 5 s connect_loopback timeout that masquerades
5	# as something else, and not as a hang that ties up CTest.
6	
7	set -euo pipefail
8	
9	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
10	. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"
11	
12	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
13	hybrid_resolve_paths
14	
15	STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
16	STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
17	SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
18	trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
19	
20	# Phase A: real QEMU 1 produces a valid entry-state snapshot so vsim has
21	# something to resume from and will reach the exit-csrwi drain. Plugin
22	# exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) on drain; see F1 in ROADMAP.md.
23	set +e
24	"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
25	  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
26	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1
27	QEMU1_RC=$?
28	set -e
29	
30	if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
31	  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
32	  exit 1
33	fi
34	
35	if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
36	  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
37	  exit 1
38	fi
39	
40	START=$(date +%s)
41	set +e
42	timeout 30 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
43	  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
44	  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
45	  --qemu-binary /nonexistent/qemu-system-riscv64 >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
46	RC=$?
47	set -e
48	ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))
49	
50	if [ "$RC" -ne 127 ]; then
51	  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: expected exit 127 (exec failure), got $RC"
52	  echo "elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"
53	  echo "--- sim log ---"
54	  cat "$SIM_LOG"
55	  exit 1
56	fi
57	
58	if [ "$ELAPSED" -gt 15 ]; then
59	  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: vsim took ${ELAPSED}s (>15s suggests a hang)"
60	  exit 1
61	fi
62	
63	# No zombie left: any leftover child of this shell would show up here.
64	if jobs -r | grep -q .; then
65	  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: background jobs still running"
66	  exit 1
67	fi
68	
69	echo "[rt_no_qemu_binary] PASS: vsim exit=$RC elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"
70

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Negative test: --qemu-binary points at a missing file. Vsim's drain must
# still fire and the spawn-fail must propagate as exit 127 (the standard
# exec-failure code), not as a 5 s connect_loopback timeout that masquerades
# as something else, and not as a hang that ties up CTest.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
hybrid_resolve_paths

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

# Phase A: real QEMU 1 produces a valid entry-state snapshot so vsim has
# something to resume from and will reach the exit-csrwi drain. Plugin
# exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) on drain; see F1 in ROADMAP.md.
set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

START=$(date +%s)
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary /nonexistent/qemu-system-riscv64 >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e
ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))

if [ "$RC" -ne 127 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: expected exit 127 (exec failure), got $RC"
  echo "elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ "$ELAPSED" -gt 15 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: vsim took ${ELAPSED}s (>15s suggests a hang)"
  exit 1
fi

# No zombie left: any leftover child of this shell would show up here.
if jobs -r | grep -q .; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: background jobs still running"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[rt_no_qemu_binary] PASS: vsim exit=$RC elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"

119	      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
120	  endif()
121	  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")
122	
123	  # End-to-end round-trip tests. Each entry registers two CTest cases
124	  # (csr + pc triggers) on the unified e2e.sh harness, which sets up an
125	  # /dev/shm-backed mmap and runs QEMU 1 + plugin -> vsim -> QEMU 2.
126	  # The harness skips (return 77) when artifacts are missing, so the
127	  # same CTestTestfile works whether ctest is invoked from the
128	  # container's /work/build or from the host's build/.
129	  #
130	  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set
131	  # in e2e.sh); the *_matmul fixtures need ~120s because the GEMM
132	  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim before drain.
133	  set(VSIM_E2E_FIXTURES
134	    # Asm fixtures (no C runtime). Shared mmap is not strictly required
135	    # for these but the unified harness uses it anyway -- the ~20 ms
136	    # mktemp cost is negligible vs the ~15-20s per-test wall.
137	    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
138	    "rt_pc_jump:0"
139	    "rt_sign_bits:0"
140	    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
141	    "rt_long_roi:0"
142	    "rt_all_gprs:0"
143	    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
144	    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
145	    "rt_csr_survival:0"
146	    "rt_shared_mem:0"
147	    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/
148	    # libgloss/handler). The C stack and heap must survive the
149	    # vsim -> QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
150	    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM.
151	    "rt_c_hello:0"
152	    "rt_c_printf:0"
153	    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
154	    # Regression for the "no redundant ELF reload" rule: .bss survival
155	    # across QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 without users hand-allocating fixed
156	    # addresses. Fails today because vsim's elf_loader and QEMU 2's
157	    # -kernel both zero-fill the .bss tail of every PT_LOAD.
158	    "rt_c_static_buf:0"
159	    # 1D 3-tap FIR convolution as a real RVV use case spanning the
160	    # handoff: QEMU1 generates x via intrinsics; vsim applies the
161	    # kernel (vslide1up / vslide1down + vadd); QEMU2 stores y and
162	    # scalar-verifies. V regfile transport rides on the save-all /
163	    # reload-all helpers in runtime/v_regfile_xfer.{h,c}.
164	    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
165	    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in the
166	    # vsim phase, verifies in QEMU2. Exercises Tier-A+B restore.
167	    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
168	    # 16x16x16 RVV GEMM under vsim, scalar recompute under QEMU 2.
169	    "rt_c_v_matmul:0:120"
170	  )
171	  foreach(entry ${VSIM_E2E_FIXTURES})
172	    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
173	    list(GET parts 0 fname)
174	    list(GET parts 1 erc)
175	    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
176	    set(timeout_env "")
177	    set(tcost 60)
178	    if(nparts GREATER 2)
179	      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
180	      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
181	      # ctest -j greedily schedules highest-cost first to keep the tail
182	      # short. Use the per-fixture timeout as the cost so long fixtures
183	      # (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave.
184	      set(tcost ${tsec})
185	    endif()
186	    add_test(
187	      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
188	      COMMAND bash -c
189	        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/e2e.sh"
190	    )
191	    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
192	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
193	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
194	      COST ${tcost}
195	    )
196	    add_test(
197	      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
198	      COMMAND bash -c
199	        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/e2e.sh"
200	    )
201	    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
202	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
203	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
204	      COST ${tcost}
205	    )
206	  endforeach()
207	
208	  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
209	  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
210	  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
211	  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
212	  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
213	  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
214	    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
215	      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
216	    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
217	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
218	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
219	    )
220	  endforeach()
221	
222	  # F-T2.4: mcycle/minstret monotonicity across the QEMU1 -> vsim handoff.
223	  # Reuses e2e.sh with CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC=1; the harness reads both
224	  # drain files (mcycle@624, minstret@632) and asserts strictly
225	  # increasing. Fixture is handoff_roundtrip (fastest asm path that
226	  # exercises the full QEMU1 -> vsim drain).
227	  add_test(
228	    NAME test_e2e_mcycle_monotonicity
229	    COMMAND bash -c

279	  # plus the centerpiece SimPoint pipeline. Each demo wraps an existing

283	  # has a higher cost: profile pass + 2 phase drains + 2 vsim replays.

file.content:
# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_offset PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_offset ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_offset)

vsim_add_test(test_state_loader tests/hybrid/state_loader.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_loader PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_loader)

vsim_add_test(test_abstract_command tests/hybrid/abstract_command.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_abstract_command)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_driver tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_driver PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_driver)

vsim_add_test(test_insn_match tests/hybrid/insn_match.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_insn_match PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_insn_match)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_site tests/hybrid/handoff_site.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_site PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_site)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_from_qemu tests/hybrid/resume_from_qemu.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_from_qemu PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_from_qemu)

vsim_add_test(test_memory_map tests/hybrid/memory_map.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_memory_map)

vsim_add_test(test_csr_trigger tests/hybrid/csr_trigger.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_csr_trigger PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_csr_trigger)

vsim_add_test(test_state_drain tests/hybrid/state_drain.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_drain PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_drain)

vsim_add_test(test_gdbstub_client tests/hybrid/gdbstub_client.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_gdbstub_client)

vsim_add_test(test_qemu_handback tests/hybrid/qemu_handback.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_qemu_handback PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_qemu_handback)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_controller tests/hybrid/handoff_controller.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_controller PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_controller)

vsim_add_test(test_icount_trigger tests/hybrid/icount_trigger.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_icount_trigger PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_icount_trigger)

vsim_add_test(test_v_regfile_xfer tests/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_v_regfile_xfer PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_v_regfile_xfer)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # End-to-end round-trip tests. Each entry registers two CTest cases
  # (csr + pc triggers) on the unified e2e.sh harness, which sets up an
  # /dev/shm-backed mmap and runs QEMU 1 + plugin -> vsim -> QEMU 2.
  # The harness skips (return 77) when artifacts are missing, so the
  # same CTestTestfile works whether ctest is invoked from the
  # container's /work/build or from the host's build/.
  #
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set
  # in e2e.sh); the *_matmul fixtures need ~120s because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim before drain.
  set(VSIM_E2E_FIXTURES
    # Asm fixtures (no C runtime). Shared mmap is not strictly required
    # for these but the unified harness uses it anyway -- the ~20 ms
    # mktemp cost is negligible vs the ~15-20s per-test wall.
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/
    # libgloss/handler). The C stack and heap must survive the
    # vsim -> QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
    # Regression for the "no redundant ELF reload" rule: .bss survival
    # across QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 without users hand-allocating fixed
    # addresses. Fails today because vsim's elf_loader and QEMU 2's
    # -kernel both zero-fill the .bss tail of every PT_LOAD.
    "rt_c_static_buf:0"
    # 1D 3-tap FIR convolution as a real RVV use case spanning the
    # handoff: QEMU1 generates x via intrinsics; vsim applies the
    # kernel (vslide1up / vslide1down + vadd); QEMU2 stores y and
    # scalar-verifies. V regfile transport rides on the save-all /
    # reload-all helpers in runtime/v_regfile_xfer.{h,c}.
    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in the
    # vsim phase, verifies in QEMU2. Exercises Tier-A+B restore.
    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
    # 16x16x16 RVV GEMM under vsim, scalar recompute under QEMU 2.
    "rt_c_v_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_E2E_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    set(tcost 60)
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
      # ctest -j greedily schedules highest-cost first to keep the tail
      # short. Use the per-fixture timeout as the cost so long fixtures
      # (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave.
      set(tcost ${tsec})
    endif()
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # F-T2.4: mcycle/minstret monotonicity across the QEMU1 -> vsim handoff.
  # Reuses e2e.sh with CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC=1; the harness reads both
  # drain files (mcycle@624, minstret@632) and asserts strictly
  # increasing. Fixture is handoff_roundtrip (fastest asm path that
  # exercises the full QEMU1 -> vsim drain).
  add_test(
    NAME test_e2e_mcycle_monotonicity
    COMMAND bash -c
      "TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3 CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC=1 bash ../tests/hybrid/e2e.sh"
  )
  set_tests_properties(test_e2e_mcycle_monotonicity PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
    COST 30
  )

  # Contract test: QEMU plugin exits 200 on drain (matches vsim's
  # kExitHandoffToQemu). Guards the F1 rebrand from regressing back to
  # exit(0), which would make workload-completion indistinguishable from
  # a drained handoff under the orchestrator.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_drain_exit_code
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_drain_exit_code.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_drain_exit_code PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # F5: plugin icount-trigger mode. Runs a CSR-less spin fixture with
  # icount=N; asserts drain at exit 200 and minstret >= N.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_icount_trigger
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_icount_trigger.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_icount_trigger PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # F7: plugin BBV-emit mode (SimPoint profile). Runs the CSR-less spin
  # with bbv_out= + slice= + icount=; asserts the .bb file has the
  # expected row count and is consumable by tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_bbv_emit
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_bbv_emit.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_bbv_emit PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # F11: plugin BBV slice-consume mode. Runs the CSR-less spin with
  # slice= + slice_at=K + icount=fallback; asserts drain at the end of
  # slice index K (0-based), before the icount fallback fires.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_phase_consume
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_phase_consume.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_phase_consume PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # Switching-mode demo suite (T1-T7): one labeled demo per M1-M8 mode,
  # plus the centerpiece SimPoint pipeline. Each demo wraps an existing
  # harness or chains plugin + simpoint + orchestrator into a single
  # user-facing flow. Labeled "switching-mode" so contributors can run
  # the subset with `ctest -L switching-mode`. T7 (full simpoint loop)
  # has a higher cost: profile pass + 2 phase drains + 2 vsim replays.
  set(VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS
    "demo_csr_oneshot:30"
    "demo_pc_oneshot:30"
    "demo_icount_roundtrip:60"
    "demo_bbv_profile:30"
    "demo_slice_consume_roundtrip:60"
    "demo_archive_replay:60"
    "demo_simpoint_loop:180"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 dname)
    list(GET parts 1 dcost)
    add_test(NAME test_${dname}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/${dname}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_${dname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;switching-mode"
      COST ${dcost}
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

function(vsim_add_test TARGET SOURCE)
  add_executable(${TARGET} EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL ${SOURCE})
  target_include_directories(${TARGET} SYSTEM PRIVATE ${VSIM_INCLUDE_DIRS})
  target_include_directories(${TARGET} PRIVATE
    "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}"
    "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/src"
    "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/src/utils")
  target_include_directories(${TARGET} SYSTEM PRIVATE
    "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/external/doctest/doctest")
  target_compile_options(${TARGET} PRIVATE ${VSIM_COMPILE_FLAGS})
  target_compile_definitions(${TARGET} PRIVATE ${VSIM_COMPILE_DEFS})
  target_link_options(${TARGET} PRIVATE ${VSIM_LINK_FLAGS})
  add_test(NAME ${TARGET} COMMAND $<TARGET_FILE:${TARGET}>)
endfunction()

vsim_add_test(test_memory_region src/tests/platform/memory_region.test.cpp)
vsim_add_test(test_optparse src/tests/utils/optparse.test.cpp)
vsim_add_test(test_cpu_signal_registry src/tests/utils/cpu_signal_registry.test.cpp)
vsim_add_test(test_sizeconvert src/tests/utils/sizeconvert.test.cpp)
vsim_add_test(test_simple_memory src/tests/platform/simple_memory.test.cpp)
vsim_add_test(test_mmap_backing src/tests/platform/mmap_backing.test.cpp)
vsim_add_test(test_interconnect src/tests/platform/interconnect.test.cpp)
vsim_add_test(test_duplicator src/tests/utils/duplicator.test.cpp)
vsim_add_test(test_kanata_logger_loopgate
  tests/pipeview/kanata_logger_loopgate.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_kanata_logger_loopgate PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_kanata_logger_loopgate ext_fmt ext_spdlog)

target_link_libraries(test_sizeconvert PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_sizeconvert ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)

input.old_string:
if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # End-to-end round-trip tests. Each entry registers two CTest cases
  # (csr + pc triggers) on the unified e2e.sh harness, which sets up an
  # /dev/shm-backed mmap and runs QEMU 1 + plugin -> vsim -> QEMU 2.
  # The harness skips (return 77) when artifacts are missing, so the
  # same CTestTestfile works whether ctest is invoked from the
  # container's /work/build or from the host's build/.
  #
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set
  # in e2e.sh); the *_matmul fixtures need ~120s because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim before drain.
  set(VSIM_E2E_FIXTURES
    # Asm fixtures (no C runtime). Shared mmap is not strictly required
    # for these but the unified harness uses it anyway -- the ~20 ms
    # mktemp cost is negligible vs the ~15-20s per-test wall.
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/
    # libgloss/handler). The C stack and heap must survive the
    # vsim -> QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
    # Regression for the "no redundant ELF reload" rule: .bss survival
    # across QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 without users hand-allocating fixed
    # addresses. Fails today because vsim's elf_loader and QEMU 2's
    # -kernel both zero-fill the .bss tail of every PT_LOAD.
    "rt_c_static_buf:0"
    # 1D 3-tap FIR convolution as a real RVV use case spanning the
    # handoff: QEMU1 generates x via intrinsics; vsim applies the
    # kernel (vslide1up / vslide1down + vadd); QEMU2 stores y and
    # scalar-verifies. V regfile transport rides on the save-all /
    # reload-all helpers in runtime/v_regfile_xfer.{h,c}.
    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in the
    # vsim phase, verifies in QEMU2. Exercises Tier-A+B restore.
    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
    # 16x16x16 RVV GEMM under vsim, scalar recompute under QEMU 2.
    "rt_c_v_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_E2E_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    set(tcost 60)
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
      # ctest -j greedily schedules highest-cost first to keep the tail
      # short. Use the per-fixture timeout as the cost so long fixtures
      # (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave.
      set(tcost ${tsec})
    endif()
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # F-T2.4: mcycle/minstret monotonicity across the QEMU1 -> vsim handoff.
  # Reuses e2e.sh with CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC=1; the harness reads both
  # drain files (mcycle@624, minstret@632) and asserts strictly
  # increasing. Fixture is handoff_roundtrip (fastest asm path that
  # exercises the full QEMU1 -> vsim drain).
  add_test(
    NAME test_e2e_mcycle_monotonicity
    COMMAND bash -c
      "TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3 CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC=1 bash ../tests/hybrid/e2e.sh"
  )
  set_tests_properties(test_e2e_mcycle_monotonicity PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
    COST 30
  )

  # Contract test: QEMU plugin exits 200 on drain (matches vsim's
  # kExitHandoffToQemu). Guards the F1 rebrand from regressing back to
  # exit(0), which would make workload-completion indistinguishable from
  # a drained handoff under the orchestrator.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_drain_exit_code
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_drain_exit_code.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_drain_exit_code PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # F5: plugin icount-trigger mode. Runs a CSR-less spin fixture with
  # icount=N; asserts drain at exit 200 and minstret >= N.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_icount_trigger
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_icount_trigger.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_icount_trigger PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # F7: plugin BBV-emit mode (SimPoint profile). Runs the CSR-less spin
  # with bbv_out= + slice= + icount=; asserts the .bb file has the
  # expected row count and is consumable by tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_bbv_emit
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_bbv_emit.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_bbv_emit PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # F11: plugin BBV slice-consume mode. Runs the CSR-less spin with
  # slice= + slice_at=K + icount=fallback; asserts drain at the end of
  # slice index K (0-based), before the icount fallback fires.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_phase_consume
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_phase_consume.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_phase_consume PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # Switching-mode demo suite (T1-T7): one labeled demo per M1-M8 mode,
  # plus the centerpiece SimPoint pipeline. Each demo wraps an existing
  # harness or chains plugin + simpoint + orchestrator into a single
  # user-facing flow. Labeled "switching-mode" so contributors can run
  # the subset with `ctest -L switching-mode`. T7 (full simpoint loop)
  # has a higher cost: profile pass + 2 phase drains + 2 vsim replays.
  set(VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS
    "demo_csr_oneshot:30"
    "demo_pc_oneshot:30"
    "demo_icount_roundtrip:60"
    "demo_bbv_profile:30"
    "demo_slice_consume_roundtrip:60"
    "demo_archive_replay:60"
    "demo_simpoint_loop:180"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 dname)
    list(GET parts 1 dcost)
    add_test(NAME test_${dname}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/${dname}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_${dname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;switching-mode"
      COST ${dcost}
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

-  # increasing. Fixture is handoff_roundtrip (fastest asm path that
-  # exercises the full QEMU1 -> vsim drain).
-  # Contract test: QEMU plugin exits 200 on drain (matches vsim's
-  # kExitHandoffToQemu). Guards the F1 rebrand from regressing back to
-  # exit(0), which would make workload-completion indistinguishable from
-  # a drained handoff under the orchestrator.
-  # F5: plugin icount-trigger mode. Runs a CSR-less spin fixture with
-  # icount=N; asserts drain at exit 200 and minstret >= N.
-  # F7: plugin BBV-emit mode (SimPoint profile). Runs the CSR-less spin
-  # with bbv_out= + slice= + icount=; asserts the .bb file has the
-  # expected row count and is consumable by tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint.
-  # F11: plugin BBV slice-consume mode. Runs the CSR-less spin with
-  # slice= + slice_at=K + icount=fallback; asserts drain at the end of
-  # slice index K (0-based), before the icount fallback fires.
-  # Switching-mode demo suite (T1-T7): one labeled demo per M1-M8 mode,
-  # plus the centerpiece SimPoint pipeline. Each demo wraps an existing
-  # harness or chains plugin + simpoint + orchestrator into a single
-  # user-facing flow. Labeled "switching-mode" so contributors can run
-  # the subset with `ctest -L switching-mode`. T7 (full simpoint loop)
-  # has a higher cost: profile pass + 2 phase drains + 2 vsim replays.

endif()

  # Verilator custom commands (unless --no-verilator)
  if(NOT VSIM_NO_VERILATOR)
    vsim_add_verilated_cpu("${CPU_NAME}")
  endif()

  vsim_add_cpu_executable(sim_${CPU_NAME} src/main.cpp)
  list(APPEND ALL_SIM_TARGETS sim_${CPU_NAME})

  if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
    vsim_add_cpu_executable(dmi_smoke_${CPU_NAME} tests/hybrid/dmi_smoke.cpp
                            EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL)
    vsim_add_cpu_executable(handoff_smoke_${CPU_NAME}
                            tests/hybrid/handoff_smoke.cpp EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL)
  endif()
endforeach()

# ##############################################################################
# Tests
# ##############################################################################
enable_testing()

function(vsim_add_test TARGET SOURCE)
  add_executable(${TARGET} EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL ${SOURCE})

file.content:
# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_offset PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_offset ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_offset)

vsim_add_test(test_state_loader tests/hybrid/state_loader.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_loader PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_loader)

vsim_add_test(test_abstract_command tests/hybrid/abstract_command.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_abstract_command)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_driver tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_driver PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_driver)

vsim_add_test(test_insn_match tests/hybrid/insn_match.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_insn_match PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_insn_match)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_site tests/hybrid/handoff_site.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_site PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_site)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_from_qemu tests/hybrid/resume_from_qemu.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_from_qemu PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_from_qemu)

vsim_add_test(test_memory_map tests/hybrid/memory_map.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_memory_map)

vsim_add_test(test_csr_trigger tests/hybrid/csr_trigger.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_csr_trigger PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_csr_trigger)

vsim_add_test(test_state_drain tests/hybrid/state_drain.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_drain PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_drain)

vsim_add_test(test_gdbstub_client tests/hybrid/gdbstub_client.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_gdbstub_client)

vsim_add_test(test_qemu_handback tests/hybrid/qemu_handback.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_qemu_handback PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_qemu_handback)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_controller tests/hybrid/handoff_controller.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_controller PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_controller)

vsim_add_test(test_icount_trigger tests/hybrid/icount_trigger.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_icount_trigger PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_icount_trigger)

vsim_add_test(test_v_regfile_xfer tests/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_v_regfile_xfer PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_v_regfile_xfer)

content.text:
The user wants me to fix the `-M virt` → `andes_ae350` mismatch (the F0 pre-existing breakage). The e2e is still running in background — I'll work on the fix in parallel and answer the question.

**Why `-M virt`:** historical default in those 5 scripts. The fixtures got re-linked at `0x00100000` for `andes_ae350` DRAM (per `tests/fixtures/test.ld:5-7`), but these scripts kept `-M virt -bios none -kernel ...`, where virt expects DRAM at `0x80000000`. Result: QEMU never reaches the CSR trigger and the drivers hang. The 3 baseline-fail tests + `test_plugin_drain_exit_code` + `probe_vcsr_drain` all share this root cause.

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Negative test: --qemu-binary opens the gdbstub port but never responds to
# packets. Vsim's gdbstub Session must time out on the read side and exit
# non-zero promptly, not block forever waiting for an ack that never comes.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"
STUB="$SCRIPT_DIR/qemu_silent_stub.py"

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
hybrid_resolve_paths

if [ ! -e "$STUB" ]; then
  echo "skip: $STUB not present"
  exit 77
fi

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

# Real QEMU 1 produces a valid entry-state snapshot so vsim reaches the
# exit-csrwi drain and tries to spawn the (silent) handback peer. Plugin
# exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) on drain; see F1 in ROADMAP.md.
set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

START=$(date +%s)
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$STUB" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e
ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))

if [ "$RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$RC" -eq 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: expected non-zero non-200 exit, got $RC"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ "$RC" -eq 124 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: bash 30 s timeout fired -> vsim hung"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ "$ELAPSED" -gt 25 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: elapsed=${ELAPSED}s (>25s suggests hang)"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[rt_gdbstub_timeout] PASS: vsim exit=$RC elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Reproduces the SIGTTIN hang of QEMU 2 when vsim is launched from a
3	# non-foreground process group under a real pty.
4	#
5	# Without the spawn_qemu stdin redirect:
6	#   - timeout(1) places vsim+QEMU 2 in a new pgid that is NOT the pty's
7	#     foreground group.
8	#   - QEMU 2's -nographic stdio reads the pty, the kernel delivers SIGTTIN,
9	#     QEMU 2 stops, vsim's waitpid blocks, the outer timeout fires (rc=124).
10	#
11	# With the fix (child stdin -> /dev/null in spawn_qemu): no tty access,
12	# no SIGTTIN, vsim completes the round-trip normally (rc=3).
13	
14	set -euo pipefail
15	
16	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
17	. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"
18	
19	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
20	hybrid_resolve_paths
21	
22	if ! command -v script >/dev/null 2>&1; then
23	  echo "skip: script(1) not available"
24	  exit 77
25	fi
26	
27	STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
28	STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
29	SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
30	RCFILE=$(mktemp)
31	WRAPPER=$(mktemp --suffix=.sh)
32	chmod +x "$WRAPPER"
33	trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG" "$RCFILE" "$WRAPPER"' EXIT
34	
35	# QEMU1 plugin drains state then exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu).
36	set +e
37	"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
38	  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
39	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1
40	QEMU1_RC=$?
41	set -e
42	
43	if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
44	  echo "FAIL[rt_pty_handback]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
45	  exit 1
46	fi
47	
48	if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
49	  echo "FAIL[rt_pty_handback]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
50	  exit 1
51	fi
52	
53	# WRAPPER captures vsim's own exit into RCFILE so the test does not depend
54	# on script(1) propagating it. timeout(1) puts vsim+QEMU 2 in a fresh pgid
55	# that is NOT the pty's foreground group, which is the exact configuration
56	# that triggers the SIGTTIN/SIGTTOU stop on QEMU 2's tty access.
57	cat >"$WRAPPER" <<EOF
58	#!/usr/bin/env bash
59	set +e
60	timeout 20 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \\
61	  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \\
62	  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \\
63	  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
64	echo \$? >"$RCFILE"
65	EOF
66	
67	START=$(date +%s)
68	script -qc "$WRAPPER" /dev/null >/dev/null
69	ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))
70	
71	VSIM_RC=$(cat "$RCFILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
72	if [ "$VSIM_RC" != "3" ]; then
73	  echo "FAIL[rt_pty_handback]: expected vsim exit 3, got '$VSIM_RC' (elapsed ${ELAPSED}s)"
74	  echo "--- sim log ---"
75	  cat "$SIM_LOG"
76	  exit 1
77	fi
78	
79	echo "[rt_pty_handback] PASS: vsim exit=$VSIM_RC under pty (elapsed ${ELAPSED}s)"
80

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Reproduces the SIGTTIN hang of QEMU 2 when vsim is launched from a
# non-foreground process group under a real pty.
#
# Without the spawn_qemu stdin redirect:
#   - timeout(1) places vsim+QEMU 2 in a new pgid that is NOT the pty's
#     foreground group.
#   - QEMU 2's -nographic stdio reads the pty, the kernel delivers SIGTTIN,
#     QEMU 2 stops, vsim's waitpid blocks, the outer timeout fires (rc=124).
#
# With the fix (child stdin -> /dev/null in spawn_qemu): no tty access,
# no SIGTTIN, vsim completes the round-trip normally (rc=3).

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
hybrid_resolve_paths

if ! command -v script >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "skip: script(1) not available"
  exit 77
fi

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
RCFILE=$(mktemp)
WRAPPER=$(mktemp --suffix=.sh)
chmod +x "$WRAPPER"
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG" "$RCFILE" "$WRAPPER"' EXIT

# QEMU1 plugin drains state then exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu).
set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_pty_handback]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_pty_handback]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

# WRAPPER captures vsim's own exit into RCFILE so the test does not depend
# on script(1) propagating it. timeout(1) puts vsim+QEMU 2 in a fresh pgid
# that is NOT the pty's foreground group, which is the exact configuration
# that triggers the SIGTTIN/SIGTTOU stop on QEMU 2's tty access.
cat >"$WRAPPER" <<EOF
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set +e
timeout 20 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \\
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \\
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \\
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
echo \$? >"$RCFILE"
EOF

START=$(date +%s)
script -qc "$WRAPPER" /dev/null >/dev/null
ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))

VSIM_RC=$(cat "$RCFILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ "$VSIM_RC" != "3" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_pty_handback]: expected vsim exit 3, got '$VSIM_RC' (elapsed ${ELAPSED}s)"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[rt_pty_handback] PASS: vsim exit=$VSIM_RC under pty (elapsed ${ELAPSED}s)"

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end roundtrip harness: QEMU 1 + plugin -> vsim -> QEMU 2.
#
# Memory between QEMU and vsim is shared via an /dev/shm-backed mmap
# (128 MB). Works for both asm fixtures (handoff_roundtrip, rt_*) and
# C-level fixtures (rt_c_*). C fixtures require shared mmap so the
# stack/heap survives handoffs; asm fixtures don't need it but run
# fine with it.
#
# Env:
#   FIXTURE         REQUIRED. .elf name under tests/fixtures/ (no .elf suffix).
#   EXPECT_RC       Final exit code to assert (default 0).
#   TRIGGER_MODE    csr | pc (default csr).
#   TIMEOUT         vsim leg timeout in seconds (default 60).
#   CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC=1   assert mcycle/minstret strictly increased
#                              between QEMU 1's drain and vsim's drain.
#   VSIM_BIN, VSIM_QEMU, VSIM_PLUGIN, VSIM_ELF, NM  override built paths.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
. "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/paths.sh"

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:?FIXTURE env var is required (e.g. FIXTURE=rt_c_hello)}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

hybrid_resolve_paths

NM="${NM:-${HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN:+$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}}"
NM="${NM:-riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT"  | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim uses the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-e2e-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1: drain entry state"
set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --shared-mem-base 0x00000000 \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

# Opt-in: mcycle/minstret must strictly increase from QEMU 1's drain
# to vsim's drain. Offsets locked by state_abi_check.h (mcycle@624,
# minstret@632). Runs before the RC gate so the verdict is independent
# of any downstream-phase failure.
if [ "${CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC:-0}" = "1" ] && [ -s "$STATE_VSIM" ]; then
  read_u64_le() { od -A n -j "$1" -N 8 -t u8 "$2" | tr -d ' \n'; }
  Q1_MCYCLE=$(read_u64_le 624 "$STATE_QEMU")
  Q1_MINST=$(read_u64_le  632 "$STATE_QEMU")
  V_MCYCLE=$(read_u64_le  624 "$STATE_VSIM")
  V_MINST=$(read_u64_le   632 "$STATE_VSIM")
  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] qemu1: mcycle=$Q1_MCYCLE minstret=$Q1_MINST"
  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] vsim:  mcycle=$V_MCYCLE minstret=$V_MINST"
  if [ "$V_MCYCLE" -le "$Q1_MCYCLE" ]; then
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: mcycle not strictly increasing across vsim"
    exit 1
  fi
  if [ "$V_MINST" -le "$Q1_MINST" ]; then
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: minstret not strictly increasing across vsim"
    exit 1
  fi
  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] PASS: strictly increasing"
fi

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-no-qemu-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

# Phase A: real QEMU 1 produces a valid entry-state snapshot so vsim has
# something to resume from and will reach the exit-csrwi drain. Plugin
# exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) on drain; see F1 in ROADMAP.md.
set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e
toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Negative test: --qemu-binary points at a missing file. Vsim's drain must
# still fire and the spawn-fail must propagate as exit 127 (the standard
# exec-failure code), not as a 5 s connect_loopback timeout that masquerades
# as something else, and not as a hang that ties up CTest.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
hybrid_resolve_paths

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

# Phase A: real QEMU 1 produces a valid entry-state snapshot so vsim has
# something to resume from and will reach the exit-csrwi drain. Plugin
# exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) on drain; see F1 in ROADMAP.md.
set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

START=$(date +%s)
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary /nonexistent/qemu-system-riscv64 >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e
ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))

if [ "$RC" -ne 127 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: expected exit 127 (exec failure), got $RC"
  echo "elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ "$ELAPSED" -gt 15 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: vsim took ${ELAPSED}s (>15s suggests a hang)"
  exit 1
fi

# No zombie left: any leftover child of this shell would show up here.
if jobs -r | grep -q .; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: background jobs still running"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[rt_no_qemu_binary] PASS: vsim exit=$RC elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"

content.text:
E2e test inventory — 45 cases total. Status of each (F0 baseline + current):

**A. Round-trip fixtures (17 fixtures × 2 modes = 34 cases) — `tests/e2e.sh` harness**

QEMU machine: `andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem` (set in `tests/e2e.sh:75`). All PASS.

| # | Fixture | Family | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `handoff_roundtrip` | asm | CSR-trigger smoke, EXPECT_RC=3 |
| 2 | `rt_pc_jump` | asm | PC-mode jump test |
| 3 | `rt_sign_bits` | asm | sign-bit boundary |
| 4 | `rt_abi_aliases` | asm | x0–x31 / ABI alias parity |
| 5 | `rt_long_roi` | asm | long ROI |
| 6 | `rt_all_gprs` | asm | full GPR survival |
| 7 | `rt_mem_loadstore` | asm | mem ld/st |
| 8 | `rt_unaligned_lh` | asm | unaligned half-word |
| 9 | `rt_csr_survival` | asm | M-mode CSR survival |
| 10 | `rt_shared_mem` | asm | shared mmap path |
| 11 | `rt_c_hello` | C | newlib stdio in phase 1+3 |
| 12 | `rt_c_printf` | C | printf survival |
| 13 | `rt_c_matmul` | C | TIMEOUT=120 |
| 14 | `rt_c_v_regs` | C+RVV | 1D FIR conv |
| 15 | `rt_c_v_csrs` | C+RVV | vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart |
| 16 | `rt_c_v_matmul` | C+RVV | TIMEOUT=120 |
| 17 | `rt_c_static_buf` | C | .bss survival |

**B. Negative drivers (4 cases, neg mode) — `tests/drivers/`**

| # | Driver | Status | Machine | Root cause |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 18 | `rt_corrupt_state.sh` | **PASS** | n/a (no QEMU) | clean |
| 19 | `rt_no_qemu_binary.sh` | **FAIL** (F0 baseline-fail) | `-M virt -kernel` | ae350-linked fixture vs virt expectation |
| 20 | `rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh` | **FAIL** | `-M virt -kernel` | same |
| 21 | `rt_pty_handback.sh` | **FAIL** | `-M virt -kernel` | same |

**C. Switching-mode demos (7 cases, demo mode) — `tests/demos/`**

All PASS. Each uses `andes_ae350` (set in test_plugin_*/e2e.sh patterns).

| # | Demo | Mode | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| 22 | `demo_csr_oneshot.sh` | M1+M8 | wraps e2e.sh trigger=csr |
| 23 | `demo_pc_oneshot.sh` | M2 | wraps e2e.sh trigger=pc |
| 24 | `demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` | M3 | plugin icount + vsim --hybrid-icount |
| 25 | `demo_bbv_profile.sh` | M4 | bbv_out + simpoint cluster |
| 26 | `demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh` | M5+M3 | slice-consume entry + icount exit |
| 27 | `demo_archive_replay.sh` | M6+M7 | orchestrator archive/replay |
| 28 | `demo_simpoint_loop.sh` | T7 | full M4→M5→M6→M7 |

**D. NOT in `run_e2e.sh`, but live under `tests/plugin/`** — registered separately in `verilator/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` before F4, now orphan; intended to be re-registered or driven manually.

| # | Plugin script | Machine | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| - | `test_plugin_drain_exit_code.sh` | `-M virt -kernel` | **broken** (same root cause) |
| - | `test_plugin_icount_trigger.sh` | `andes_ae350` | PASS |
| - | `test_plugin_bbv_emit.sh` | `andes_ae350` | PASS |
| - | `test_plugin_phase_consume.sh` | `andes_ae350` | PASS |
| - | `probe_vcsr_drain.sh` | `-M virt -kernel` | **broken** |

**Summary of `-M virt` users (the fix target):**
- 3 in `run_e2e.sh` (the F0 fail set): `rt_no_qemu_binary`, `rt_gdbstub_timeout`, `rt_pty_handback`
- 2 not in `run_e2e.sh`: `test_plugin_drain_exit_code`, `probe_vcsr_drain`

All 5 use `handoff_roundtrip.elf` / `probe_v_csr.elf` (both ae350-linked), so the switch from `-M virt -bios none -kernel` to `-machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem -bios` should resolve the QEMU1 stall in each. Want me to proceed with the edit on all 5?

123:Fixture source lives in `verilator/hybrid/test/`. C-level fixtures link `runtime/{crt0.S, trap.S, handler.c}` plus the Andes toolchain's `-lgloss_vh` (semihosting-backed libgloss), using `rt_c.ld`. V-using fixtures require `-march=rv64gcv`. **Phase-2 rule:** the span between `enter_vsim()` and `exit_vsim()` runs on vsim and MUST NOT issue any syscall (no `printf`, `fopen`, semihosting ebreak, etc.) -- vsim does not implement semihosting, so any libgloss-routed call inside the ROI traps. Phase 1 and phase 3 print via standard newlib stdio (both QEMUs are launched with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`).

content.content:
audit of `qemu_plugin/`, `verilator/src/hybrid/`,
`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`, and the test suite at
`verilator/tests/hybrid/`.

Format: one row per failure mode.

---
  `runtime/trap.S` falls into `handler.c` which calls `_exit(mcause)`,
  itself a semihosting ebreak. Infinite trap loop / test timeout.
- Source: `docs/vsim_semihosting_investigation.md` (unsolved);
  `verilator/hybrid/test/runtime/{trap.S,handler.c}`
- Fix: Add a host-side semihosting handler inside vsim so libgloss_vh
  keeps working unchanged across the ROI. Pre-scan the ELF for the
---
  `demo_bbv_profile.sh`, `demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`,
  `demo_archive_replay.sh`, `demo_simpoint_loop.sh` only assert exit
  code + PC range. A wrong register value passes all of them.
- Source: `verilator/tests/hybrid/demo_*.sh`
- Fix: In each demo, after QEMU2 connects via gdbstub, issue a single
  `g` packet to read the full register file; parse and memcmp byte
---
  truncated to wrong size; corrupt magic with valid struct size.
- Breaks: Existing negative drivers cover only 4 scenarios
  (corrupt-state-short-read, no-qemu-binary, gdbstub-timeout,
  pty-handback). Subtle resource-exhaustion or race-condition paths
  unguarded.
- Source: `verilator/tests/hybrid/rt_{corrupt_state,no_qemu_binary,gdbstub_timeout,pty_handback}.sh`
- Fix: Add three negative drivers: `rt_fault_partial_drain.sh`
  (truncate state.bin to half its size mid-handoff),
  `rt_fault_disk_full.sh` (run on a size-capped tmpfs smaller than
  state size), `rt_fault_mmap_resize.sh` (truncate the shared mmap
  while vsim is mid-execution). Each asserts a clean diagnostic exit.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Negative test: --qemu-binary points at a missing file. Vsim's drain must
# still fire and the spawn-fail must propagate as exit 127 (the standard
# exec-failure code), not as a 5 s connect_loopback timeout that masquerades
# as something else, and not as a hang that ties up CTest.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
hybrid_resolve_paths

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

# Phase A: real QEMU 1 produces a valid entry-state snapshot so vsim has
# something to resume from and will reach the exit-csrwi drain. Plugin
# exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) on drain; see F1 in ROADMAP.md.
set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

START=$(date +%s)
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary /nonexistent/qemu-system-riscv64 >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e
ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))

if [ "$RC" -ne 127 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: expected exit 127 (exec failure), got $RC"
  echo "elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ "$ELAPSED" -gt 15 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: vsim took ${ELAPSED}s (>15s suggests a hang)"
  exit 1
fi

# No zombie left: any leftover child of this shell would show up here.
if jobs -r | grep -q .; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: background jobs still running"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[rt_no_qemu_binary] PASS: vsim exit=$RC elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Negative test: --qemu-binary opens the gdbstub port but never responds to
# packets. Vsim's gdbstub Session must time out on the read side and exit
# non-zero promptly, not block forever waiting for an ack that never comes.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"
STUB="$SCRIPT_DIR/qemu_silent_stub.py"

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
hybrid_resolve_paths

if [ ! -e "$STUB" ]; then
  echo "skip: $STUB not present"
  exit 77
fi

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

# Real QEMU 1 produces a valid entry-state snapshot so vsim reaches the
# exit-csrwi drain and tries to spawn the (silent) handback peer. Plugin
# exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) on drain; see F1 in ROADMAP.md.
set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

START=$(date +%s)
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$STUB" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e
ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))

if [ "$RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$RC" -eq 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: expected non-zero non-200 exit, got $RC"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ "$RC" -eq 124 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: bash 30 s timeout fired -> vsim hung"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ "$ELAPSED" -gt 25 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: elapsed=${ELAPSED}s (>25s suggests hang)"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[rt_gdbstub_timeout] PASS: vsim exit=$RC elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Negative test: --qemu-binary opens the gdbstub port but never responds to
# packets. Vsim's gdbstub Session must time out on the read side and exit
# non-zero promptly, not block forever waiting for an ack that never comes.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"
STUB="$SCRIPT_DIR/qemu_silent_stub.py"

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
hybrid_resolve_paths

if [ ! -e "$STUB" ]; then
  echo "skip: $STUB not present"
  exit 77
fi

SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-gdbstub-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

# Real QEMU 1 produces a valid entry-state snapshot so vsim reaches the
# exit-csrwi drain and tries to spawn the (silent) handback peer. Plugin
# exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) on drain; see F1 in ROADMAP.md.
set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

START=$(date +%s)
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$STUB" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e
ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))

if [ "$RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$RC" -eq 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: expected non-zero non-200 exit, got $RC"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ "$RC" -eq 124 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: bash 30 s timeout fired -> vsim hung"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ "$ELAPSED" -gt 25 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: elapsed=${ELAPSED}s (>25s suggests hang)"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[rt_gdbstub_timeout] PASS: vsim exit=$RC elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"
